blocks:
Ayahs

طسٓمٓ (۱)
Ta, Seen, Meem. (1)
تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُبِينِ (۲)
These are the signs of the Manifest Book. (2)
لَعَلَّكَ بَـٰخِعٌ نَّفْسَكَ أَلَّا يَكُونُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ (۳)
You are liable to imperil your life [out of distress] that they will not have faith. (3)

Key Points

Much has been said about the meaning of the disconnected letters (حروف مقطّعه). Perhaps the best explanation is that by presenting these letters at the beginning of some Surahs of the Quran (29 Surahs), Allah intends to convey that the Quran, His eternal miracle, is composed of these very letters. If you can, bring forth a book comparable to it using these same Arabic alphabet letters. The first Ayah of Surah Ash-Shura, which follows the disconnected letters and states: «كذلك یُوحى» (Thus He reveals), confirms this view. The word «باخِع» (bākhī‘) means destroyer or killer.

Messages

1- A comprehensive school of thought must possess a strong, decisive, written, and systematic logic, in such a way that everyone has access to it. «تِلك آیاتُ الكتابِ المبین»

2- The Quran holds a very lofty status. «تلك» («Tilk» is a demonstrative pronoun for the distant, indicating a very high status.)

3- Wherever you reach a dead end, the Quran is your illuminator. «الكتابِ المبین»

4- Repetition is one of the principles of education (tarbiyah). (The Ayah «تلك آیاتُ الكتابِ المبین» is repeated following the disconnected letters at the beginning of Surahs Yusuf, Qasas, and Ash-Shu'ara.)

5- Effort and burning zeal are not effective everywhere. «لعلّك باخِع...»

6- Consoling sorrowful individuals is a divine act. «تلك آیات... لعلّك باخع»

7- The Prophets strove beyond expectation to fulfill their duty. «لعلّك باخِع»

8- One of the prominent characteristics of the Prophets is burning zeal (sūz). «باخِع نفسك» (Zeal is a sign of love for the school of thought and the community/Ummah.)

9- If the ground for influence is not present, no result or effect will be obtained even from the best book and the best teacher. «اَلاّ یكونوا مؤمنین»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ash-Shu'ara

This Surah has 227 Ayahs, revealed in Mecca, and contains the most Ayahs after Surah Al-Baqarah. This Surah is named "Ash-Shu'ara" (The Poets) because of its praise for believing poets and its condemnation of frivolous poets, which appears in its last four Ayahs. Its other name is "Ta Sin Mim" (طسم). The stories of Prophets such as Moses, Abraham, Noah, Lot, Salih, Hud, and Shu'ayb علیهم السلام, and the stubborn confrontations of the people with them, are recounted in this Surah. At the end of each story, Allah says: «اِنّ فى ذلك لآیة و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین و اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز الحكیم». Therefore, this Ayah is repeated eight times to console the Prophet of Islam and tell Him not to worry about the stubbornness of the people of Mecca, because all the Prophets faced such people. Indeed, history is the best means for strengthening the morale of the believers, increasing insight, broadening patience (sa'at-e sadr), and providing foresight against the threats of enemies. Therefore, the leaders of society must be aware of the history of society.

Ayah

إِن نَّشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ ءَايَةً فَظَلَّتْ أَعْنَـٰقُهُمْ لَهَا خَـٰضِعِينَ (٤)
If We wish We will send down to them a sign from the sky before which their heads will remain bowed in humility. (4)

Messages

1- The tradition (Sunnah) and law of Allah is granting people free will and respite. «اِن نشأ»

2- Paying attention to Divine power is the best means for the soul's tranquility. «لعلّك باخع... اِن نشأ»

3- Faith must be voluntary and chosen, not compulsory. «فظلّت اعناقهم لها خاضعین»

Ayahs

وَمَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن ذِكْرٍ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ مُحْدَثٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ عَنْهُ مُعْرِضِينَ (٥)
There does not come to them any new reminder from the All-beneficent but that they disregard it. (5)
فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا۟ فَسَيَأْتِيهِمْ أَنۢبَـٰٓؤُا۟ مَا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٦)
They have already denied [the truth], but soon there will come to them the news of what they have been deriding. (6)

Key Points

In these verses, three characteristics are mentioned for the disbelievers (kuffār): turning away (i‘rāḍ), denial (takdhīb), and mockery (istihzā). Yes, man is gradually and step-by-step drawn towards downfall. First, he shows indifference and turns away from the truth, then he denies it, and finally, he mocks the truth. But the worst state and characteristic of man is mocking the truth. Therefore, this verse states: In the future, the news (consequences) of their mockery will reach them. And it does not say: The punishment for turning away and denial will reach them.

Messages

1- The Quran was revealed gradually. «ما یَأتیهم مِن ذكر... مُحدث»

2- The reminders in the Quran are one of the permanent favors (luṭf) of Allah. «ذكر مِن الرّحمن»

3- No reminder is effective for hard-hearted individuals. «من ذكرٍ»

4- Allah completes the argument (itmām al-ḥujjah) against the disbelievers through reminders, repetition, and novelty. «ذكر... محدث»

5- Man should not be so attached to his ancient traditions that he disbelieves every new word he hears, even if it is the truth. «ذكر... محدث... عنه معرضین»

6- Religion is a factor of awareness, not sedation. «من ذكر»

7- Instead of accepting the truth, a group of people have already chosen the path of obstinacy. (Turning away and distancing oneself from the truth is the permanent method of a group of people). «كانوا»

8- Turning away (i‘rāḍ) that stems from arrogance (istikbār) and denial (takdhīb) is dangerous. «معرضین - كذّبوا»

9- Indifference to future dangers and punishments is the secret of Divine wrath. «انباؤا ما كانوا»

10- Those who mock the truth and its followers, their actions will not go unanswered. «ما كانوا به یستهزؤن» (They will see the consequence of their evil deeds either in this world, or at the time of death, or in Barzakh, or during the Return (Raj‘ah), or on the Day of Judgment.)

Ayahs

أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَمْ أَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍ كَرِيمٍ (۷)
Have they not regarded the earth, how many of every splendid kind [of vegetation] We have caused to grow in it? (7)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۸)
There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (8)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۹)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (9)

Key Points

In the middle of the eighteenth century AD, "Linné," the famous Swedish botanist, succeeded for the first time in discovering the issue of pairing (sexuality) in some plants. He said: masculinity and femininity also exist in plants, and fertilization is necessary for plants to bear fruit. The Noble Qur'an considers the law of pairing (duality) to include plants and non-plants.(1)

1) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- Studying the creatures of existence and their beauties is the best way to know Allah. «أوَلم یروا»

2- The Qur'an reproaches those who do not look at the world with deep insight. «أوَلم یروا الى الارض»

3- One of the signs of Allah's power is the pairing (duality) of plants. «اَنبتنا... زوج كریم»

4- There are benefits in all plants, even if the benefit of some may not yet have been discovered by us.(2) «من كلّ زوج كریم»

5- If a person lacks the capacity and readiness to accept the truth, he will not learn a lesson even from the entirety of existence. «أوَلم یروا ... ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

6- Majority is not always a sign of truthfulness (or righteousness). «ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

7- The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is under the special care of Allah. «اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز الرّحیم»

8- Allah's Mercy is not based on weakness; He is Merciful while being Invincible, and He grants respite to opponents so that they might return and repent. «العزیز الرّحیم»

2) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi.

Ayahs

وَإِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبُّكَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱئْتِ ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٠)
When your Lord called out to Moses: [saying,] ‘Go to those wrongdoing people, (10)
قَوْمَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۚ أَلَا يَتَّقُونَ (۱۱)
the people of Pharaoh. Will they not be wary [of Allah]?’ (11)
قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ أَن يُكَذِّبُونِ (۱۲)
He said, ‘My Lord! I fear they will impugn me, (12)

Key Points

In this Surah, this phrase is repeated seven times: «ألا تتّقون», «ألا یتّقون». Yes, if there is no piety (Taqwa), the words of the prophets are not effective.

Messages

1- The history of the prophets must not be forgotten. («اذ» means remember that time.)

2- Both the goal and the conduct (Sīrah) of the prophets are similar, and the reactions of the opponents of the prophets are also uniform. Therefore, familiarity with their history can be a guide for the Prophet of Islam. «واذ نادى ربّك»

3- Sending prophets to the people is among the affairs (Shu'ūn) of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «نادى ربّك»

4- Struggling against the tyrant (Taghut) is at the forefront of the prophets' programs. «اِئتِ القوم الظالمین»

5- In enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), sometimes one must face danger. «أن ائت القوم الظالمین»

6- The uprising of the prophets is based on standards (criteria), not malice or revenge. «القوم الظالمین»

7- Forgetting Allah and following the tyrant (Taghut) is the greatest injustice (Dhulm). «القوم الظالمین»

8- For forbidding the evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), go to the sources of corruption. «قوم فرعون»

9- Tyrants (Taghuts) have no power without the help of the people. «قوم فرعون»

10- The lack of piety (Taqwa) among the people is the ground for the growth of tyrants (Taghuts). «الا یتّقون»

11- Guiding the people is a heavy burden that requires Divine assistance. «ربّ انّى أخاف...»

Ayahs

وَيَضِيقُ صَدْرِى وَلَا يَنطَلِقُ لِسَانِى فَأَرْسِلْ إِلَىٰ هَـٰرُونَ (۱۳)
and I will become upset and my tongue will fail me. So send for Aaron [to join me]. (13)
وَلَهُمْ عَلَىَّ ذَنۢبٌ فَأَخَافُ أَن يَقْتُلُونِ (۱٤)
Besides, they have a charge against me, and I fear they will kill me.’ (14)
قَالَ كَلَّا ۖ فَٱذْهَبَا بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ ۖ إِنَّا مَعَكُم مُّسْتَمِعُونَ (۱٥)
He said, ‘Certainly not! Let both of you go with Our signs: We will indeed be with you, hearing [everything]. (15)
فَأْتِيَا فِرْعَوْنَ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٦)
So approach Pharaoh and say, ‘‘We are indeed envoys of the Lord of the worlds (16)
أَنْ أَرْسِلْ مَعَنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (۱۷)
that you let the Children of Israel leave with us.’’ ’ (17)

Key Points

The intent behind the sin of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) mentioned in Ayah 14 refers to the incident alluded to in Surah Al-Qasas, Ayah 15: Before his prophethood, Moses entered a city and saw one of his followers engaged in a fight with one of the (Copts), the companions of Pharaoh. Moses' follower asked him for help, and Moses, in support of him, struck the Copt with a punch, which caused him to die. This incident created a stir in the region, forcing Moses to flee. Although this act of Moses was not intended to kill the person, but rather to support an oppressed individual, it caused Moses to gain the reputation of a killer among the Pharaohites. When Moses attained the rank of prophethood, he said to Allah: I fear that the incident of the killing of that Coptic man will impede my mission. (It is inferred from the word "كلاّ" [Never/By no means] that Moses' act was not premeditated murder, otherwise Allah would not say to a deliberate murderer: It is nothing, I assure you of My support.)

Messages

1- The capacity of individuals, even Prophets, is limited. (Except for Allah, everyone and everything is limited). «یَضیق صَدرى»

2- When accepting responsibility, it is necessary for a person to state his weaknesses. «یَضیقُ صدرى»

3- Frankness and honesty are prominent characteristics of the Prophets. «یَضیقُ صَدرى»

4- Leadership, guidance, propagation, and instruction require broad-mindedness (patience/tolerance). «و یَضیق صَدرى»

5- In forbidding evil and engaging in struggle, sometimes one must move as a group of two or more. «فاذهبا»

6- Compensate for weaknesses, but do not shirk responsibility. «یَضیق صَدرى ولایَنطلق لسانى فَأرسل الى هارون» (My breast straitens, and my tongue is not fluent, so send to Aaron). Accepting or granting great responsibilities is permissible despite weaknesses and shortcomings that can be compensated for.

7- Sound and unbiased individuals introduce other worthy people to accept great responsibilities. «فَأرسل الى هارون»

8- The past records of individuals affect their success. «و لهم عَلىَّ ذَنب»

9- The law of retribution (Qisas) has a very long history. «ولهم عَلىَّ ذَنب فاخاف اَن یَقتلون» (And they have a charge against me, so I fear that they will kill me). (Martyrdom is the aspiration of the Prophets, but their fear and concern was that they might not achieve their sacred goals.)

10- Management of propagation requires several important matters, including: studying social conditions, foresight, and preparing effective grounds for propagation. «اَخاف - لهم علىّ ذنب - فارسل الى هارون»

11- In stating the truth, fear neither the scarcity of the supporters of truth nor the abundance of its opponents. «فاذهبا»

12- Allah accepts useful and appropriate suggestions. «فاَرسَل الى هارون ... فاذهبا»

13- Consoling and helping those who are worried is a Divine act. «انّا معكم»

14- Allah is the supporter of the Prophets. «انّا معكم»

15- Precise and moment-by-moment awareness is a necessity in important missions. «انّا معكم مستمعون»

16- The miracles of the Prophets are tools for guidance and instruction, and their provisions for the nations. «بآیاتنا»

17- Allah is aware of the details of our actions. «مستمعون» (Listening). Allah is present and observing everywhere.

18- To combat organized and corrupt systems, first go to the source. «فأتیا فرعون»

19- Be frank and firm in propagating and inviting others to the truth. «انّا رسول ربّ العالمین»

20- Sending a Messenger to the people is among the affairs of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah). «رسول ربّ»

21- All existence has a form of growth. «ربّ العالمین»

22- All existence is under His management. «ربّ العالمین»

23- All Prophets share one goal. «انّا رسول» (Indeed, we are the Messenger) and He did not say: «انّا رسولا» (Indeed, we are two Messengers).

24- In response to someone who says: «أنَا ربّكم الاَعلى» (I am your Lord, the Most High) (3), one must say: «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the worlds).

25- If two Prophets are commissioned at the same time, the leadership belongs to one of them. «انّا رسول»

26- Freeing people from the captivity of tyrants is at the forefront of the Prophets' programs. «اَرسِل مَعنا بنى اسرائیل»

(3) An-Nazi'at, 24.

Ayahs

قَالَ أَلَمْ نُرَبِّكَ فِينَا وَلِيدًا وَلَبِثْتَ فِينَا مِنْ عُمُرِكَ سِنِينَ (۱۸)
He [i.e. Pharaoh] said, ‘Did we not rear you as a child among us, and did you not stay with us for years of your life? (18)
وَفَعَلْتَ فَعْلَتَكَ ٱلَّتِى فَعَلْتَ وَأَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۹)
Then you committed that deed of yours, and you are an ingrate.’ (19)

Key Points

Pharaoh questioned the prophethood of Moses using two statements: A: You are a murderer among us, and a murderer cannot be a prophet. B: We raised you; how can you deny and be ungrateful for Our favors? An ungrateful person cannot be a prophet. The subsequent verses state Moses' response.

Messages

1- Imposing favors (or reminding others of them) and showing off are characteristics of the arrogant (Mustakbirūn). «ألم نُربّك فینا»

2- The Divine Will raised Moses in the lap of Pharaoh. «ألم نُربّك»

3- If we raise someone, or teach him a profession, or help him with marriage, housing, or similar matters, we should not use this as a reason or excuse to reject his rightful speech. «ألم نُربّك فینا ولیداً»

4- The friends of Allah (Awliyā' Allah) are not bound by obligation (of gratitude), and the environment and society do not affect them. «لَبِثتَ فینا من عُمُرِك سنین» (Moses did not say, "Since we have been at Pharaoh's table for a long time, or since everyone is now submissive to him and society revolves around him, let us compromise a little and remain silent.")

5- The right of bread and salt (obligation of gratitude) is not that we abandon the person who has the right (to guidance) and accept his mistakes. (Prophet Moses had eaten Pharaoh's bread and salt, but he did not abandon him.) «نربّك فینا... لَبثتَ فینا»

6- We should not pay attention to the undue expectations of the arrogant. Ingratitude towards the arrogant is not bad. «و انت من الكافرین»

Ayahs

قَالَ فَعَلْتُهَآ إِذًا وَأَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ (۲٠)
He said, ‘I did that when I was astray. (20)
فَفَرَرْتُ مِنكُمْ لَمَّا خِفْتُكُمْ فَوَهَبَ لِى رَبِّى حُكْمًا وَجَعَلَنِى مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۲۱)
So I fled from you, as I was afraid of you. Then my Lord gave me sound judgement and made me one of the apostles. (21)

Key Points

The meaning of al-hukm (judgment/rule) in the Ayah is either the governance and guardianship of the Children of Israel or wisdom and knowledge. In these two Ayahs, Prophet Moses عليه السلام responds to the objections of Pharaoh, which we mentioned in the previous Ayah.

As mentioned, Prophet Moses, due to supporting one of his followers, struck his opponent with a punch, and the opponent died from that very blow. This killing was neither intentional nor carried out with a murder weapon; rather, it was an unintended incident and a practical slip without malicious intent, which occurred before Moses's prophethood, and specifically because he was defending an oppressed person. Therefore, dhaall (astray) in the mentioned Ayah refers to an unintentional practical deviation that does not harm infallibility ('ismah), or it means bewilderment, similar to Ayah 7 of Surah Ad-Duha: «و وَجَدك ضالاً فَهَدى».

Amir al-Mu'minin Ali عليه السلام was asked: Why did you not take your right with the sword after the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and why did you not fight the Caliphs who preceded you in the same way you fought Talhah, Zubayr, and Mu'awiyah? He replied: Sometimes silence is necessary. Did not Abraham say to his people: I withdraw from you: «و اعتزلُكم و ما تَدعون من دون اللّه»(4) Did not Aaron say: «اِنّ القوم استضعَفونى و كادوا یَقتلونَنى»(5) The people weakened me and were about to kill me? Did not Joseph say: «ربّ السِجنُ اَحبّ الىّ»(6) My Lord! Prison is dearer to me than responding to their request? Did not the Holy Prophet go into the cave, and did I not sleep in his place? Did not Moses say: «ففَررتُ منكم لما خِفتكم» I fled from you when I feared you? And did not Prophet Lot عليه السلام, in response to the demand of the sinners, say: «لو اَنّ لى بكم قُوّة او آوى الى رُكنٍ شدید»(7) If only I had power over you (to stop you from this shameful act) or could seek refuge in a strong and secure support (to be safe from your evil)?(8) Therefore, other friends of Allah were also sometimes forced into silence and isolation under certain circumstances.

4) Maryam, 48. 5) A'raf, 150. 6) Yusuf, 33. 7) Hud, 83. 8) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Sometimes confession is a virtue. «قال فَعَلتُها»

2- Fleeing from the tyrant is a prelude to receiving divine favors. «ففررت منكم... فوهب»

3- Prophets benefit from the special favors of Allah. «فَوَهبَ لى - و جَعَلنى»

4- Prophethood is a continuous process throughout history; many prophets existed before Moses, so Pharaoh's astonishment is misplaced. «من المرسلین»

Ayahs

وَتِلْكَ نِعْمَةٌ تَمُنُّهَا عَلَىَّ أَنْ عَبَّدتَّ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (۲۲)
That you have enslaved the Children of Israel—is that the favour with which you reproach me?’ (22)
قَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَمَا رَبُّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۲۳)
He said, ‘And what is ‘‘the Lord of all the worlds?’’ ’ (23)

Key Points

Moses said to Pharaoh: I should have grown up in my father's house. Why did you enslave my father so that I was forced to grow up in your house? Is this a favor that I should be grateful for, or is it captivity?! Although all means of comfort were available to me in your palace, I was distressed because the Children of Israel were suffering.

It is understood from the Quran that Pharaoh knew God internally: «قال لقد عَلِمتَ ما انزل هولاء الاّ ربّ السموات والارض»(9) but outwardly he considered himself the supreme lord (Rabb A'la) of the people, like a manager who has authority over individuals but simultaneously considers himself subordinate to another.(10)

9) Al-Isra, 102.

10) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Arrogance makes ugliness appear beautiful to a person. «تلك نعمة تَمنّها علىَّ»

2- Respond to his arrogance and favors (boasts) with firmness. «تلك نعمة...»

3- Sometimes the arrogant feign ignorance to protect their throne. «و ما ربّ العالمین»

4- The question posed by the arrogant is proud and accompanied by the humiliation of others. Pharaoh said: «و ما ربّ العالمین» and did not say: «و مَن ربّ العالمین» (The particle "Ma" [ما] in the Arabic language is used for inanimate objects.)

Ayahs

قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ (۲٤)
He said, ‘The Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them,—should you have conviction.’ (24)
قَالَ لِمَنْ حَوْلَهُۥٓ أَلَا تَسْتَمِعُونَ (۲٥)
He said to those who were around him, ‘Don’t you hear?!’ (25)
قَالَ رَبُّكُمْ وَرَبُّ ءَابَآئِكُمُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۲٦)
He said, ‘Your Lord, and the Lord of your forefathers!’ (26)

Key Points

The Quran states: The dispute between the prophets and their opponents was over the Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah, meaning Allah's management and legislation, and the necessity of obeying His commands. Otherwise, their opponents accepted Allah as the Creator (Khāliq) of the universe. The statement of those who say today that religion is separate from politics is like the statement of those who say that the account of creating the universe by the will of Allah is separate from the account of managing it. (Let Allah create, but let us govern.) The Quran and intellect recognize the right of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah), the right to impose duties, and the right to be obeyed, as belonging only to the One who is the Creator, not to others. The One who created knows what law to legislate. «الا یعلم مَن خَلق»(11) Some have interpreted the phrase «ان كنتم موقنین» (if you are certain) in this way: Moses said, "I know that your question is not for understanding, but for evasion. But if you are truly seeking the truth, reflect upon the order and system of existence, and you will understand His Lordship and Oneness."

11) ملک، 14.

Messages

1- Clear and explicit answers must be given to theological questions. «قال و ما ربّ العالمین قال ربّ السموات والارض»

2- The entire existence has one God. «ربّ السموات والارض» (Contrary to the polytheists who believed in multiple gods.)

3- The entire existence is under one management, supervision, and evolutionary movement. «ربّ العالمین»

4- The Essence (Dhāt) of Allah is not knowable; He must be known and introduced through His creations. «ربّ السموات والارض» (Pharaoh asked about the nature and quality of God, but Moses answered by referring to His creations, because the Essence is not knowable.)

5- Not everyone can grasp the unity of management over the entire existence. «ان كنتم موقنین» People of certainty (Ahl al-Yaqīn), whenever they look deeply at the creation of the heavens and the earth, comprehend the Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah).

6- Creating a hostile atmosphere, concealing the truth, and interrupting the logical speech of others are the tools of the tyrants (Tāghūts). «اَلا تستمعون» (The debate between Moses and Pharaoh was public.)

7- Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) is uniform regarding existing humans, past humans, and the heavens and the earth. «ربّ السموات و الارض... ربّكم و ربّ آبائكم...» (Allah's management, supervision, and administration over the external signs (Āyāt Āfāqī) (the heavens and the earth) and the internal signs (Āyāt Anfusī) (humans) are uniform.)

Ayahs

قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمَجْنُونٌ (۲۷)
He said, ‘Indeed your messenger, who has been sent to you, is surely crazy!’ (27)
قَالَ رَبُّ ٱلْمَشْرِقِ وَٱلْمَغْرِبِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَآ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۲۸)
He said, ‘The Lord of the east and the west and whatever is between them—should you exercise your reason.’ (28)
قَالَ لَئِنِ ٱتَّخَذْتَ إِلَـٰهًا غَيْرِى لَأَجْعَلَنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلْمَسْجُونِينَ (۲۹)
He said, ‘If you take up any god other than me, I will surely make you a prisoner!’ (29)
قَالَ أَوَلَوْ جِئْتُكَ بِشَىْءٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳٠)
He said, ‘What if I bring you something [as an] unmistakable [proof]?’ (30)
قَالَ فَأْتِ بِهِۦٓ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۳۱)
He said, ‘Then bring it, should you be truthful.’ (31)

Key Points

In Ayah 27, we see that Pharaoh, in order to compensate for his own humiliation, used the harshest tones and the ugliest accusations against Moses. In Arabic literature, letters and words are used to emphasize a point. The word ‘inna (اِنّ), the letter lām (لام) in the word lamajnūn (لَمجنون), and the use of a nominal sentence (jumlat ismiyyah) are signs of his emphasis on this false accusation.

Messages

1- Tyrants evade the truth when confronted with it. (Every time Moses presented an argument or statement, Pharaoh, instead of responding to Moses, spoke to the people around him because he was worried about losing them.) «قال لمَن حَوله - قال اِنّ رسولكم»

2- The arrogant never accepts the truth and considers himself separate from the people. «رسولكم - ارسل الیكم» (meaning: your messenger, not mine).

3- One of the strongest weapons of the opponents of the prophets is the false accusation of madness. «لمجنون» Indeed, whoever is incapable of providing evidence resorts to slander and abuse.

4- From the harmony of the laws and the stable system governing the earth and the heavens, the East and the West, and all beings in the universe, we understand that the cosmos is managed by a single authority. «ربّ المشرق والمغرب ...»

5- The preacher and educator must not tire of discussion; he must repeat his arguments and possess patience (broad-mindedness). «ربّ السموات والارض», «ربّكم و ربّ آبائكم», «ربّ المشرق والمغرب»

6- Accusations do not divert the friends of Allah (Awliyā’ Allāh) from their goals. «رسولكم... لمجنون قال ربّ المشرق والمغرب»

7- Attaining Monotheism (Tawhid) requires intellect. (Disbelief and polytheism are the products of irrationality). «ان كنتم تعقلون» (The people's thinking is the greatest danger to the arrogant).

8- The prophets speak courteously. (Pharaoh said to Moses عليه السلام: «لمجنون», but Moses said: If you use your intellect, you will understand my words). «ان كنتم تعقلون»

9- Threatening is another weapon of the tyrants. «لأجعلنّك من المَسجونین» (Religious leaders must be prepared for hardships).

10- In a tyrannical system, suppression and violence prevail, and many individuals are imprisoned. «من المَسجونین»

11- Where logic fails to have an effect, a miracle is necessary. «بشى ء مبین»

12- Prior preparation is a condition for success and victory over the enemy. Moses knew that the staff would turn into a serpent and his hand would become luminous; therefore, he said with confidence: «جئتك بشى ء مبین»

13- The miracles of the prophets are real, and people understand them clearly. «مبین»

Ayahs

فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ ثُعْبَانٌ مُّبِينٌ (۳۲)
Thereat he threw down his staff, and behold, it was a manifest python. (32)
وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُۥ فَإِذَا هِىَ بَيْضَآءُ لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ (۳۳)
Then he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white and bright to the onlookers. (33)

Key Points

The meaning of the word naza' (نَزع) here is the act of drawing the hand out from the pocket or collar. The transformation of the staff into a serpent (dragon) is mentioned ten times, and the illumination of the hand of Moses (موسى عليه السلام) is mentioned five times in the Quran. In the first stage when Moses released the staff, it turned into a small snake, as mentioned in Surah An-Naml, Ayah 10: «جانٌّ وَلىَّ مُدبراً» (a serpent, moving swiftly). But, in the face of the rebellious tyrant (Taghut), it turned into a great serpent (dragon). This is perhaps because one must speak and act appropriately for every audience.

Messages

1- Since the miracles of the prophets depend on Divine power, they do not require practice and do not need to be gradual. «فاذا هى» (meaning the staff suddenly became a serpent).

2- In the face of rebellious individuals, a striking and overwhelming action must be performed. «ثُعبان مبین»

3- The miracles of the prophets are clear and illuminating, not illusion or fantasy. «مبین»

4- Alongside a terrifying miracle, the white and luminous hand is a symbol of purity and affection. «ثُعبان - بیضاء»

5- To ensure the effectiveness of the word of truth, utilize all correct methods of fear and hope. «ثُعبان - بیضاء»

Ayahs

قَالَ لِلْمَلَإِ حَوْلَهُۥٓ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسَـٰحِرٌ عَلِيمٌ (۳٤)
He said to the elite [who stood] around him, ‘This is indeed an expert magician (34)
يُرِيدُ أَن يُخْرِجَكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُم بِسِحْرِهِۦ فَمَاذَا تَأْمُرُونَ (۳٥)
who seeks to expel you from your land with his magic. So what do you advise?’ (35)
قَالُوٓا۟ أَرْجِهْ وَأَخَاهُ وَٱبْعَثْ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حَـٰشِرِينَ (۳٦)
They said, ‘Put him and his brother off for a while, and send heralds to the cities (36)
يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ سَحَّارٍ عَلِيمٍ (۳۷)
to bring you every expert magician.’ (37)
فَجُمِعَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ لِمِيقَـٰتِ يَوْمٍ مَّعْلُومٍ (۳۸)
So the magicians were gathered for the tryst of a known day, (38)
وَقِيلَ لِلنَّاسِ هَلْ أَنتُم مُّجْتَمِعُونَ (۳۹)
and the people were told: ‘Will you all gather?!’ (39)

Key Points

Pharaoh dealt with Moses in several ways: A: Humiliation: «ألم نُربّك فینا ولیداً» Did We not raise you among us as a child? B: Mockery: «لمجنون» You are insane. C: Threat: «من المَسجونین» I will certainly imprison you. D: Accusation of sorcery: «لساحر علیم» You are a sorcerer. E: Accusation of conspiracy: «یرید أن یخرجكم» You intend to revolt.

In the verses related to the dialogue between Moses and Pharaoh, there is no mention of Aaron, and this is because the official stance must be determined by the leader.

The intent of the phrase «فماذا تأمرون» (What do you command?) is a consultative command. This is because advisors would present their opinions in the form of an order or command. For example: «أرجِه وأخاه» Give Moses and his brother a respite and do not imprison him.

Prophet Moses and Pharaoh agreed upon the time and place for the ceremony of the sorcerers' confrontation with Moses. «لمیقات یوم معلوم» (for the appointment of a known day).

The people were asked to be present at the promised time and place, because this presence and gathering would make Moses and Aaron feel isolated, empower the sorcerers, and allow for propaganda and sensationalism.

Spineless and flattering courtiers imitate even the words used. The phrases «لساحر علیم» and «یرید ان یخرجكم من ارضكم» in these two verses are spoken by Pharaoh, but in Surah Al-A'raf, verses 109 and 110, the exact same words are spoken by his courtiers, without even adding or subtracting a single letter.

Messages

1- Tyrants express their satanic propaganda with emphasis and certainty. «اِنّ هذا لساحر علیم» (The particle Inna, the letter Lam, and the nominal sentence structure are all signs of emphasis.)

2- Pharaoh referred to Moses as a «ساحر علیم» (knowing sorcerer) in order to encourage and incite the sorcerers in their confrontation with him. «لساحرعلیم»

3- A liar has a poor memory. The Pharaohites who had previously accused Moses of madness, «لمجنون» (insane), now call him a skilled and knowledgeable sorcerer: «لساحر علیم»

4- Patriotism and the motivation for ownership are human instincts, and Pharaoh used these two subjects as pretexts to incite the people against Moses, saying: He will displace you and nullify your ownership. «یخرجكم من ارضكم» (He wants to expel you from your land).

5- Lying, deception, and demagoguery are the methods of the tyrant. (The word «ارضكم» (your land) was used for demagoguery, and the word «بسحره» (by his sorcery) was a clear lie.)

6- Tyrants, when in power and control, consider everything their own, «ألیس لى ملك مصر»(12) (Is not the dominion of Egypt mine?), but when they sense danger, they draw the people into the scene. «ارضكم» (your land).

7- Tyrants are dependent on their subordinates and seek counsel and solutions from them. «فماذا تأمرون» (What do you command?). The Pharaoh who told Moses, "If you take a god other than me, I will imprison you," is today in need of his courtiers.

8- The time of Pharaoh was an era when sorcery and magic were prevalent. «سحّار علیم» (knowing sorcerer).

9- If specialists lack commitment, they end up serving the Pharaohs. «یأتوك» (they will bring him to you).

10- Conferences and seminars for experts have a long history. «یأتوك بكلّ سحار علیم»

11- Tyrants misuse experts to achieve their goals; why should we not make good use of them? «یأتوك بكلّ سحّار علیم»

12- Pharaoh employed all his power. «بكلّ سحّار» (every sorcerer).

13- Time and place are effective in propaganda movements. «یوم معلوم» (a known day).

14- Gaining public opinion and the presence of the people on the scene is important for tyrants. «هل انتم مجتمعون»

(12) (Zukhruf, 51).

Ayahs

لَعَلَّنَا نَتَّبِعُ ٱلسَّحَرَةَ إِن كَانُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبِينَ (٤٠)
‘Maybe we will follow the magicians, if they are victors!’ (40)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالُوا۟ لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَئِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبِينَ (٤۱)
So when the magicians came, they said to Pharaoh, ‘Shall we have a reward if we were to be the victors?’ (41)
قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ إِذًا لَّمِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ (٤۲)
He said, ‘Of course; and you will be among members of my inner circle.’ (42)

Messages

1- Pharaoh claimed divinity, but with the miracle of Prophet Moses, the situation reached a point where everyone became followers of the sorcerers, and they considered the final word to be that of the victorious sorcerers. «لعلّنا نتّبع السحرة»

2- Upon seeing Moses' miracle, Pharaoh was not certain of the sorcerers' victory. «ان كانوا هم الغالبین» (The particle «اِنْ» is a conditional particle and indicates doubt.)

3- Materialistic individuals speak of money even in matters of doctrine and belief. «فلمّا جاء السحرة قالوا لفرعون أئنّ لنا لاجراً»

4- The sorcerers are concerned only with receiving a wage and pay no attention to whether the act is right or wrong. «أئنّ لنا لاجرا» The motto of the prophets in all their endeavors was «ما اسئلكم علیه من أجر»(13), but the sorcerers expected a reward even for a single action. «لنالأجراً»

5- A person who is not godly (devoted to Allah) looks toward Pharaoh's money; «لنا لاجراً» but as soon as he becomes godly, the entire existence is small in his sight, and he sacrifices his life easily. As we read in Ayah 50: «لا ضَیر انّا الى ربّنا لمنقلبون»

6- Sometimes status and prestige are more important than wealth. «انّكم اذاً لمن المقرّبین»(13) Shu'ara, 109, 127, 145, 164 and 180.

Ayahs

قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ (٤۳)
Moses said to them, ‘Throw down whatever you have to throw!’ (43)
فَأَلْقَوْا۟ حِبَالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ وَقَالُوا۟ بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ ٱلْغَـٰلِبُونَ (٤٤)
So they threw down their sticks and ropes, and said, ‘By the might of Pharaoh, we shall surely be victorious!’ (44)

Messages

1- Men of God believe in the dominance of truth and the futility of magic, and they speak from a position of strength and calmness, not out of weakness or fear. «اَلقُوا ما انتم مُلقون»

2- Give opponents the opportunity to strive and act according to their belief and goal, «اَلقُوا ما انتم», and then respond to them firmly and logically.

3- The disbeliever is internally unstable but outwardly shouts slogans. The sorcerers doubted their victory, and in previous verses, they said: «اِن كنّا نحن الغالبین». However, in Pharaoh's presence, through flattery and sycophancy, they said: «بِعزّة فرعون», pretending to be victorious and launching what is known as psychological warfare. «انّا لنحن الغالبون»

Ayahs

فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِىَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ (٤٥)
Thereat Moses threw down his staff, and behold, it was swallowing what they had faked. (45)
فَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سَـٰجِدِينَ (٤٦)
Thereat the magicians fell down prostrating. (46)
قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤۷)
They said, ‘We believe in the Lord of all the worlds, (47)
رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ (٤۸)
the Lord of Moses and Aaron.’ (48)

Key Points

The sorcerers immediately believed as soon as they saw the staff turn into a serpent. It is mentioned in narrations (Riwāyāt) that when Imam Mahdi عليه السلام appears, specialists from the East and the West will also believe in him, because they will understand the magnitude of the work better than others.

Messages

1- A miracle is a swift and sudden event, not gradual or practiced. «فاذا هى تَلقَف»

2- Falsehood cannot withstand the truth. «تَلقَفُ ما یأفكون»

3- Sorcery and magic are practical lies, far removed from reality. (The word «یأفكون» comes from ifk, meaning fabrication or lie.)

4- Those who are ready do not evade the truth; they submit. «فاُلقى»

5- Only the goldsmith knows the value of gold; only the jeweler knows the value of the jewel. (The sorcerers, who were experts, recognized the miracle as distinct from magic and were so affected that they involuntarily fell into prostration, even though the Pharaoh's people were present and did not believe.) «فاُلقى السحرة»

6- One of the privileges of humanity is the ability to change one's intellectual stance in an instant. «فاُلقى»

7- Prostration (Sujud) is the manifestation of worshipping the Truth and submission, which has a history throughout time. «ساجدین»

8- The entire existence is under the management of Allah in an evolutionary movement. «ربّ العالمین»

9- Misuse must be prevented. So that the Pharaoh's people would not interpret «ربّ العالمین» (Lord of the Worlds) in a deviated sense, the sorcerers said: «ربّ موسى و هارون» (Lord of Moses and Aaron).

10- The sorcerers affirmed the fundamentals of religion: Monotheism (Tawhid): «آمنّا بربّ العالمین»; Prophethood (Nubuwwah): «ربّ موسى و هارون»; Resurrection (Ma'ad): «انّا الى ربّنا منقلبون»

Ayahs

قَالَ ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحْرَ فَلَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ۚ لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَـٰفٍ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ (٤۹)
He [i.e. Pharaoh] said, ‘Do you profess faith in Him before I should permit you? He is indeed your chief who has taught you magic! Soon you will know! I will cut off your hands and feet from opposite sides, and I will crucify you all.’ (49)
قَالُوا۟ لَا ضَيْرَ ۖ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ (٥٠)
They said, ‘[There is] no harm [in that]! Indeed, we shall return to our Lord. (50)
إِنَّا نَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لَنَا رَبُّنَا خَطَـٰيَـٰنَآ أَن كُنَّآ أَوَّلَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٥۱)
Indeed we hope our Lord will forgive us our offences for being the first to believe.’ (51)

Key Points

As soon as the sorcerers saw the miracle of Moses (Musa), they fell to the ground and prostrated. At this point, Pharaoh, in order to deceive the masses, offered an analysis and said: This is a pre-planned conspiracy; you were students of Moses and have staged this puppet show to cause my downfall. He was oblivious to the fact that the sorcerers were scattered throughout the country and could not reach Moses to be his students, besides the fact that they had sworn by the glory of Pharaoh to achieve victory. In some exegeses (tafasir), we read: Pharaoh cut off the hands and feet of the sorcerers and hung them on tall palm trees, and Prophet Moses wept for them.

Messages

1- In a tyrannical system, people do not have freedom of belief. «آمنتم له قبل اَن آذنَ لكم»

2- Tyrants (Taghuts) want to dominate even the thoughts of the people. «قبل اَن آذنَ لكم»

3- A liar has a poor memory. «لمجنون - علّمكم السحر»

4- The worst torture that removes a person's power, efficiency, and balance is the cutting off of hands and feet from opposite sides (cross-amputation). «من خلاف»

5- Do not trust the tyrants (Taghuts); they want everything for themselves. (Pharaoh, who had promised just an hour before that he would make them among his close associates, now threatens them all with death). «لاُصلّبنكم»

6- One of the effects of belief in the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is courage in the face of tyrants. «لا ضیر لنا»

7- Belief in the Resurrection is a bright horizon for the future. «انّا الى ربّنا منقلبون»

8- Martyrdom in the way of Allah is not loss or destruction. «لا ضیر لنا»

9- Whoever's faith is based on recognition (of truth) will not waver. «لا ضیر لنا» (The sorcerers, having recognized the miracle, stood firm against Pharaoh's threats).

10- Both sorcery (sihr) is a sin and serving the Pharaohs is a sin. «خطایانا»

11- Greed (Tame') for material things is negative, but for spiritual things, it is positive. «نطمع اَن یَغفر لنا»

12- Forgiveness is among the attributes of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «یَغفر لنا ربّنا»

13- The hope for pardon must be accompanied by genuine return (to God). «آمنّا - نطمع»

14- Precedence and pioneering in faith is a virtue. «اوّل المؤمنین»

15- A good end (Husn al-'Aqibah) is the greatest felicity. Those whose entire lives were spent in deviation were transformed in an instant by a spark, «انّا الى ربّنا منقلبون», and became among the first believers. «كنّا اوّل المؤمنین»

16- Conspiracy disgraces the conspirator. (Pharaoh invested heavily and invited the sorcerers to disgrace Moses, but when the sorcerers believed, Pharaoh himself was disgraced). «كنّا اوّل المؤمنین»

Ayahs

۞ وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِىٓ إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ (٥۲)
Then We revealed to Moses, [saying],‘Set out with My servants at night, for you will be pursued.’ (52)
فَأَرْسَلَ فِرْعَوْنُ فِى ٱلْمَدَآئِنِ حَـٰشِرِينَ (٥۳)
Then Pharaoh sent heralds to the cities, (53)

Key Points

The work of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) is divided into three parts: The first part was inviting Pharaoh and manifesting miracles. The second part was the magicians believing in him and the various ups and downs (challenges). The third part was moving his followers and the believers, and their pursuit by Pharaoh and the disbelievers, which culminated in the believers crossing the sea and the disbelievers drowning. In the word «حشر» (Hashr), the meaning of rousing, driving, and gathering is inherent. The Children of Israel provided benefits for the Pharaohites, which is why Pharaoh prevented their migration. Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) was commanded to lead them out of Egypt by night.

Messages

1- After the rejection of argumentation (evidence), it is the turn of Divine wrath, which begins with the command to Moses to migrate and the pursuit by the Pharaohites, and ends with their drowning and destruction. «و اوحینا الى موسى اَن أسرِ»

2- All actions of the Prophets, even the time of migration, were through revelation. «اوحینا...»

3- To fight Pharaoh, utilize the darkness of the night as well. «أسرِ»

4- Either we reform the corrupt society or we distance ourselves from it and migrate. «أَسرِ»

5- The Prophets do not merely give advice and admonition; establishing great movements and leading them are among their other tasks. «أسرِ بعبادى»

6- One of the stages of Nahy 'anil-Munkar (forbidding the evil) is migration from a corrupt society. «أسرِ بعبادى»

7- Allah saves His servants from destruction. «أَسرِ بعبادى»

8- Tyrants (Taghuts) resort to spreading rumors and negative propaganda in the cities. «فارسل فرعون فى المدائن حاشرین»

Ayahs

إِنَّ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَشِرْذِمَةٌ قَلِيلُونَ (٥٤)
[proclaiming:] ‘These are indeed a small band. (54)
وَإِنَّهُمْ لَنَا لَغَآئِظُونَ (٥٥)
They have aroused our wrath, (55)
وَإِنَّا لَجَمِيعٌ حَـٰذِرُونَ (٥٦)
and we are alert and fully prepared.’ (56)
فَأَخْرَجْنَـٰهُم مِّن جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (٥۷)
So We took them out of gardens and springs, (57)
وَكُنُوزٍ وَمَقَامٍ كَرِيمٍ (٥۸)
and [made them leave behind] treasures and stately homes. (58)
كَذَٰلِكَ وَأَوْرَثْنَـٰهَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (٥۹)
So it was; and We bequeathed them to the Children of Israel. (59)
فَأَتْبَعُوهُم مُّشْرِقِينَ (٦٠)
Then they pursued them at sunrise. (60)

Key Points

The word shirdhimah (شِرذِمة) means a limited group that has separated from the main body. Pharaoh, in his propaganda, introduced his opponents as a small group. However, the commentaries narrate from Ibn Abbas that the number of people who moved with Prophet Musa was about 600,000, yet Pharaoh called this large population: «اِنّ هؤلاء لَشِرذمة قلیلون» (14)

The word hādhīrūn (حاذرون) comes from hadhr (حذر), meaning a defensive tool. Therefore, «اِنّا لجمیع حاذرون» means that all of us are ready and armed for revenge. The Pharaohites, in their propaganda, considered themselves a united group, «اِنّا لجمیع حاذِرون», but they called the Children of Israel qalīlūn (قَلیلون) [plural masculine], not qalīlah [singular feminine], meaning they were scattered groups, not united and cohesive, and that they (the Pharaohites) would quickly uproot and destroy them. They were unaware that the cohesion of the Children of Israel was so great that, with a single sign and command from Prophet Musa, they all migrated overnight.

14) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Kabir Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- Tyrants belittle popular movements. «لَشِرذمة قلیلون»

2- Misleading public opinion is a tyrannical method. «لَشِرذمة قلیلون»

3- Tyrants should know that they are hated. «و اِنّهم لنا لغائظون»

4- Hatred and anger towards tyrants and dissociation from them are prerequisites of faith in Allah. «و اِنّهم لنا لغائظون»

5- Palaces are not eternal; oppression is the key to the decline of power. «فاخرجناهم ...»

6- The Pharaohites possessed civilization in Egypt. «و كنوز و مقام كریم»

7- One of the Divine traditions is replacing the arrogant oppressors with the oppressed. «كذلك و اورثناها بنى اسرائیل»

8- The wealth, palaces, and gardens of the Pharaohites reached the Children of Israel. «اورثنا»

9- The Pharaohites' pursuit of the Children of Israel was a Divine plan for their drowning. «فاخرجنا - فَاتبَعوهم مُشرقین»

Ayahs

فَلَمَّا تَرَٰٓءَا ٱلْجَمْعَانِ قَالَ أَصْحَـٰبُ مُوسَىٰٓ إِنَّا لَمُدْرَكُونَ (٦۱)
When the two hosts sighted each other, the companions of Moses said, ‘Indeed we have been caught up.’ (61)
قَالَ كَلَّآ ۖ إِنَّ مَعِىَ رَبِّى سَيَهْدِينِ (٦۲)
He said, ‘Certainly not! Indeed my Lord is with me. He will guide me.’ (62)

Key Points

Tarā'ā (تَرائى) means seeing one another, and Jam‘ān (جَمعان) means two groups. Perhaps the use of "Companions of Moses" (Aṣḥāb Mūsā) instead of the Children of Israel (Bani Israel) is a sign that even the close companions of Moses had anxieties because the sea was before them and Pharaoh's army was pursuing them. Although every movement, uprising, and decision requires necessary knowledge and insight, it is not necessary for a person to be aware of all stages in the first step. Just as Prophet Moses, until the moment He saw the sea in front of Him and the enemy behind Him, did not know the plan, yet faith in Divine guidance was always within Him. Therefore, He said: «اِنّ معِىَ ربّى سیَهدین»

Messages

1- Heavenly leaders have calm hearts during crises and are a source of tranquility for others. «كلاّ اِنّ معِىَ ربّى»

2- We must have certainty in Divine promises. (In Ayah 15 of this Surah, Allah told Moses: «انّا معكم مستمعون» [Indeed, We are with you, listening]. In this Ayah, Moses tells the people: Allah promised, and He is with us). «كلاّ اِنّ معِىَ ربّى»

3- When confronting the enemy (even when the sea is on one side and the enemy army is on the other), do not despair, and rely upon Allah. «اِنّ معىَ ربّى»

4- Prophets are the medium for Allah's grace and mercy. «اِنّ معىَ ربّى سیهدین» (Considering that the Prophet's guidance is a medium for the guidance of the people.)

5- Prophets are under the special protection of Allah. «اِنّ معِىَ ربّى»

6- Guidance is among the attributes of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّى سیهدین»

Ayahs

فَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنِ ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْبَحْرَ ۖ فَٱنفَلَقَ فَكَانَ كُلُّ فِرْقٍ كَٱلطَّوْدِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (٦۳)
Thereupon We revealed to Moses: ‘Strike the sea with your staff!’ Whereupon it parted, and each part was as if it were a great mountain. (63)
وَأَزْلَفْنَا ثَمَّ ٱلْـَٔاخَرِينَ (٦٤)
There, We brought the others near, (64)
وَأَنجَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥٓ أَجْمَعِينَ (٦٥)
and We delivered Moses and all those who were with him. (65)
ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا ٱلْـَٔاخَرِينَ (٦٦)
Then We drowned the rest. (66)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (٦۷)
There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them do not have faith. (67)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٦۸)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (68)

Key Points

The word infilāq means splitting open, firq means a piece or segment, and ṭawd means mountain. It seems that as a result of the splitting of the great Nile River, which is like a sea, the water accumulated so high on both sides of the created dry path that it became like two tall mountains.

Messages

  1. The actions of the Prophets are by the will of the Lord and Divine revelation. «فاوحینا...»

  2. Allah performs actions through His chosen servants so that people pay attention to them and obey them. «فاوحینا الى موسى أنِ اضرب» (Indeed, nature and its manifestations are, by the will of Allah, under the control and subjugation of His special servants.)

  3. By the will of Allah, a staff one day turns into a serpent, causing fear among the enemies, and another day becomes the key to mercy and the opening of a path for the believers.

  4. By the will of Allah, a staff sometimes causes water to gush from a stone: «اِضرب بعصاك الحجر»(15) and sometimes causes the sea to dry up. «اِضرب بعصاك البحر»

  5. The entire existence and all its actions and reactions are by the will of Allah. «أوحینا - أزلفنا - أنجینا - أغرقنا»

  6. Salvation and prosperity are achieved by accompanying the Prophets. «أنجینا موسى ومن معه»

  7. Allah rescues His supporters from dead ends even in the most difficult circumstances. «أنجینا موسى ومن معه»

  8. The splitting of the sea and the drowning of the Pharaohites are signs of Divine power and wrath, and a source of admonition. «اِنّ فى ذلك لآیةً»

  9. The Divine tradition throughout history is the support of truth and the suppression of falsehood. «لآیة» Indeed, a historical event serves as a sign and a lesson when that event, or its equivalent, is repeatable throughout history.

  10. By recounting the history of past nations, Allah offers solace and teaches patience to the Prophet of Islam. «ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

  11. Those who accept admonition are few. «لآیة و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

  12. The Prophet of Islam is under the special protection of Allah. «ربّك» (This is addressed to the Prophet, meaning that just as We destroyed the opponents of Moses, We can destroy your opponents with the same might and power.)

  13. The disbelief of people does not harm the Might of Allah. «ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین - اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز الرّحیم»

  14. A nurturer must be both powerful and merciful. «اِنّ ربّك لهوالعزیز الرّحیم»

  15. The respite that Allah grants to the disbelievers is not due to weakness or inability; it is based on mercy and compassion, so that perhaps they may repent. «العزیز الرّحیم»

(15) Baqarah, 60.

Ayahs

وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ (٦۹)
Relate to them the account of Abraham (69)
إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِۦ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ (۷٠)
when he said to his father and his people, ‘What is it that you are worshiping?!’ (70)
قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ أَصْنَامًا فَنَظَلُّ لَهَا عَـٰكِفِينَ (۷۱)
They said, ‘We worship idols, and are constant in our devotion to them.’ (71)
قَالَ هَلْ يَسْمَعُونَكُمْ إِذْ تَدْعُونَ (۷۲)
He said, ‘Do they hear you when you call them? (72)
أَوْ يَنفَعُونَكُمْ أَوْ يَضُرُّونَ (۷۳)
Or do they bring you any benefit, or cause you any harm?’ (73)
قَالُوا۟ بَلْ وَجَدْنَآ ءَابَآءَنَا كَذَٰلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ (۷٤)
They said, ‘Indeed, we found our fathers doing likewise.’ (74)
قَالَ أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا كُنتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ (۷٥)
He said, ‘Have you regarded what you have been worshipping, (75)
أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُمُ ٱلْأَقْدَمُونَ (۷٦)
you and your ancestors? (76)

Key Points

After Prophet Moses, whose name appears more than 130 times in the Quran, the name of Prophet Abraham appears more often than other prophets in the Quran.

Based on narrations, the meaning of the word "أَب" (father) in this verse refers to Azar, Abraham's uncle. This is because it is impossible for the father of a prophet like Abraham to be an unbeliever (Kafir). Linguistically, "أَب" is also used for a guardian, grandfather, and father-in-law. In Ayah 133 of Surah Al-Baqarah, we read that the sons of Jacob said to their father: "نَعبُد الهك و اله آبائك ابراهیم و اسماعیل و اسحاق" (We shall worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael, and Isaac). Although Jacob is the son of Isaac, this verse refers to both the grandfather (Abraham) and the uncle (Ishmael) as "أَب."

Imitation (Taqlid) of the people of knowledge and scholars is sometimes a means of perfection, growth, and progress; "فسئلوا اهل الذكر" (16) and "فبِهُداهم اقتده..." (17). But imitation of the common people is a means of stagnation and rigidity. "انّا وَجَدنا آباءنا" (Indeed, we found our fathers [doing this]), because unquestioning adherence to national and tribal customs and traditions causes the stupefaction of society, blocks the path of thought and innovation, and prevents the recognition of truth.

Since Prophet Abraham is praised by all Jewish and Christian groups, relating his history will be instructive for the People of the Book during the time of the Prophet, as well as for Muslims.

The word "رأیتم" (you have seen/considered) comes from the root "رأى" and "رؤیت" (seeing). Therefore, "رأیتم" includes both seeing with the eye and contemplating with the intellect.

16) Nahl, 43; Anbiya, 7.

17) An'am, 90.

Messages

1- The Prophet of Islam is commissioned to preserve the history of the prophets who preceded him. "واتلُ علیهم" (Recite to them).

2- The life story of Prophet Abraham عليه السلام is important and beneficial. ("نبأ" refers to important news).

3- The traditions and laws governing history are constant. Therefore, the history of yesterday can be a lesson for today. "واتلُ علیهم نبأ ابراهیم" (Recite to them the news of Abraham).

4- In forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar), we should start with our own kin. "لابیه" (to his father).

5- In forbidding evil, age is not a condition. "قال لابیه" (He said to his father).

6- In forbidding evil, power is not a condition. "قال لابیه و قومه" (He said to his father and his people). (Considering that at that time, Prophet Abraham was in a situation where his uncle had rejected him.)

7- In forbidding evil, do not fear loneliness. "قال لابیه و قومه" (He said to his father and his people).

8- In defending the truth, do not show undue consideration for family. "قال لابیه" (He said to his father).

9- By stating that Abraham's closest relatives were idolaters and did not obey him, Allah consoles the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. "لابیه و قومه - نعبد اصناما" (to his father and his people – We worship idols).

10- In the method of propagation (Tabligh), awaken consciences through questioning. "ما تعبدون" (What do you worship?). (Although Abraham عليه السلام saw what they were worshipping, he questioned them to compel them to think and awaken their consciences.)

11- Man is a worshipping being; if he does not worship the truth, he will turn to falsehood. "ما تَعبدون - نَعبُد اصناما" (What do you worship? – We worship idols).

12- In relating history, the Noble Quran highlights sensitive and constructive points. "ما تعبدون" (What do you worship?). (In the historical accounts it relates, the Quran does not refer to numbers, names, time, or place, but rather conveys the instructive message and content.)

13- In forbidding evil, start with the most important evils. "ما تعبدون" (What do you worship?). (Yes, the most important evil is polytheism (Shirk) toward Allah.)

14- Not every act of worship is valuable; the type of deity worshipped and the degree of awareness are important. "نعبد اصناماً" (We worship idols).

15- Worship is not a momentary desire but is rooted deep in the human soul. "عاكفین" (steadfastly devoted).

16- Worse than deviation is boasting about deviation. "فنظلّ لها عاكفین" (and remain devoted to them). ("نظلّ" means we always worship idols, and "عاكفین" means we are constantly adhering to their worship.)

17- Argumentation and logic are the first steps in propagation. "هل یَسمعونكم - او یَنفعونكم" (Do they hear you? – or do they benefit you?).

18- The motivation for worship is either bringing benefit or repelling harm, and idols possess neither. "ینفعونكم او یضرّون" (benefit you or harm you).

19- Use clear and understandable arguments with ordinary people. "هل یَسمعونكم اذ تدعون او یَنفعونكم او یَضرّون" (Do they hear you when you call upon them, or do they benefit you or harm you?).

20- In matters of belief (Aqa'id), imitation (Taqlid) is forbidden. "وَجَدنا آبائنا" (We found our fathers).

21- Sometimes the customs and traditions of ancestors are an obstacle to reason and logic. "وَجدنا آبائنا" (We found our fathers).

22- Precedent is not proof of righteousness. "وَجدنا آبائنا" (We found our fathers).

23- Even idolaters believe in the ineffectiveness of idols; they are merely trapped by their ancestors. "بل وَجَدنا آبائنا" (Nay, but we found our fathers).

24- Fanaticism and tribalism are forbidden. "وَجدنا آبائنا" (We found our fathers).

25- Ignorance and naivety are the foundation and context for deviation. "أفرأیتم ماكنتم تعبدون" (Have you considered what you worship?). (Polytheism has no scientific or rational basis.)

26- Contemplation and reasoning are a necessity for man. "أفرأیتم ما كنتم تعبدون" (Have you considered what you worship?).

27- Debate, discussion, and argumentation are necessary among different viewpoints. "أفرأیتم ماكنتم..." (Have you considered what you were...).

Ayahs

فَإِنَّهُمْ عَدُوٌّ لِّىٓ إِلَّا رَبَّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۷۷)
They are indeed enemies to me, but the Lord of all the worlds, (77)
ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَنِى فَهُوَ يَهْدِينِ (۷۸)
who created me, it is He who guides me, (78)
وَٱلَّذِى هُوَ يُطْعِمُنِى وَيَسْقِينِ (۷۹)
and provides me with food and drink, (79)
وَإِذَا مَرِضْتُ فَهُوَ يَشْفِينِ (۸٠)
and when I get sick, it is He who cures me; (80)
وَٱلَّذِى يُمِيتُنِى ثُمَّ يُحْيِينِ (۸۱)
who will make me die, then He will bring me to life, (81)
وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَطْمَعُ أَن يَغْفِرَ لِى خَطِيٓـَٔتِى يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ (۸۲)
and who, I hope, will forgive me my faults on the Day of Retribution.’ (82)

Key Points

Except for Allah, Who is the Lord of the Worlds, all other objects of worship become our enemies, because idols will speak on the Day of Judgment and complain against the idolaters. As the Quran states: «كلاّ سیَكفرون بعبادَتهم و یكونون علیهم ضِدّا»(18) On the Day of Judgment, the idols will detest the idolaters and be hostile towards them, thereby revealing their enmity.(19) The error of the prophets is tark al-awla (leaving the preferable), otherwise Allah has praised Prophet Abraham with attributes such as the chosen one. «اِنّا اَخلصناهم بخالصَة ذِكرَى الدّار»(20)

(18) Maryam, 82. (19) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi. (20) Sad, 46.

Messages

  1. Imaginary and false objects of worship are the enemies of human thought, happiness, and perfection. «عدوّ لى...»
  2. One must first pay attention to the world and the Creator of the world, and then pay attention to oneself. «ربّ العالمین - خلقنى»
  3. In the method of propagation, it is sometimes necessary to put oneself in the place of others. «عدوّ لى» (my enemy) is used, and He did not say: «عدوّ لكم» (your enemy).
  4. The creation of man is one of the greatest divine favors. «الّذى خلقنى»
  5. Only He who has created has the right to guide. «خلقنى فهو یهدین» because planning and guidance are inseparable from creation.
  6. Creating and guiding is a sign that He loves us, not the idols. «عدوّ لى الاّ ربّ العالمین الّذى خلقنى فهو یهدین»
  7. Guidance is among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّ العالمین - فهو یَهدین»
  8. The blessing of guidance (after creation) is the greatest of blessings. «خلقنى فهو یَهدین»
  9. Creation is a one-time event, but guidance is constant and continuous. («خلقنى» is in the past tense, and «یهدین» is in the present/continuous tense).
  10. Feeding and quenching man’s thirst is the work of Allah. Water and bread are merely means. «یُطعِمنى و یَسقین»
  11. Attention to spiritual blessings precedes attention to material blessings. First, He said: «فهو یَهدین ...», and then He said: «یُطعِمنى و یَسقین ...»
  12. We are responsible for the onset of illness. «مَرِضتُ» (I became sick) is used, and He did not say: «أمرَضَنى» (He made me sick).
  13. Healing is from Allah. Medicine is a means, and its effect is also by His will. «فهو یَشفین»
  14. The healing of the soul and the healing of the body are necessary side by side. «فهو یهدین - فهو یَشفین»
  15. In speech and oration, the rhythmic quality of words is valuable. «یَهدین - یَسقین - یَشفین - یُحیین»
  16. In the divine worldview, death is not the end; it is one stage of life. «یُمیتنى ثم یُحیین»
  17. Allah is the sole agent of existence (Tawhid al-Af'al / Unity of Acts). «یَهدین - یَسقین - یَشفین - یُحیین»
  18. Death is also a blessing. «والّذى یُمیتنى...» Death is listed alongside blessings.
  19. Wherever there is a potential for polytheism (Shirk), the elimination of Shirk is necessary. (The word «هو» (He) is used three times regarding the blessings of guidance, feeding, and healing, but it is not used regarding creation, death, and life. This is perhaps because everyone attributes creation and death to Allah, and there is no room for Shirk there. However, guidance, feeding, and healing are often attributed to people, medicine, doctors, and food. Therefore, the Quran used the word «هو» to keep the spirit of Monotheism alive, meaning all affairs are in His hands).
  20. One of the philosophies of worship is thanking Allah. (Abraham asked his uncle and his people: «ما تعبدون» What do you worship? They said: Idols. In these verses, Prophet Abraham explains the philosophy of worshipping Allah). «خلقنى - یهدین - یُطعمنى - یَسقین - یَشفین - یُمیتنى و یُحیین»
  21. No one is independent of divine favors. «اطمع اَن یَغفر...» If Abraham, the Friend of Allah (Khalil Allah), considers himself in need of divine forgiveness, our duty is clear.
  22. Divine pardon is His grace, not our entitlement. «اَطمع» (I hope). Yes, we should not rely on our deeds, because Abraham also looked toward divine pardon, not the reward for his own work.
  23. Hope and expectation (Raja') are valuable. «اَطمع»
  24. The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah causes attachment to His pardon and forgiveness. «ربّ العالمین... اَطمع اَن یَغفر لى»
  25. Seeking forgiveness is the way of the prophets. «یَغفرلى»
  26. If His special grace is not present, man, regardless of his status, is prone to error. «خطیئتى»
  27. The manifestation of the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad) is evident in the speech of the prophets. «خَلقنى... یوم الدین»
  28. Man's material and spiritual needs, from the beginning to the end of life, are in the hands of Allah. «خلقنى... یوم الدّین»

Ayah

رَبِّ هَبْ لِى حُكْمًا وَأَلْحِقْنِى بِٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۸۳)
‘My Lord! Grant me [unerring] judgement, and unite me with the Righteous. (83)

Key Points

The word hukm (حُكم) can mean governance, guardianship, and management of affairs, or it can mean knowledge and insight. However, hukm has a superior meaning to hikmah (wisdom), because hikmah is merely the power to distinguish truth from falsehood, while hukm is awareness coupled with readiness to implement the truth. (21)

Human felicity is summarized in a few phrases found in the supplication of Prophet Ibrahim (عليه السلام): A: Knowing Allah and having inner insight regarding Him. «هَب لى حُكماً» B: Presence in a righteous society. «اَلحِقنى بالصالحین» C: A good name throughout history. «لسان صِدقٍ فى الاخرین» D: Attaining eternal Paradise. «ورثة جنّة النّعیم»

21) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- To attain perfection, man must seek aid from Allah. «ربّ هَب لى...»

2- Seeking aid using the Name Rabb (Lord) is effective in the acceptance of supplication. «ربّ»

3- Wisdom and insight coupled with action is a Divine gift. «هَب لى حُكما»

4- Before making a new request to Allah, mention His previous blessings and thank Him. «خَلَقنى - یَهدین - یَشفین - یَسقین - و... ربّ هَب لى»

5- If wisdom exists, but a person's friends are unworthy, wisdom will not be effective. «هَب لى حُكما و ألحقنى»

6- If vision, insight, and correct thinking exist, then behavior, speech, and intention will also be correct. (Wisdom [hikmah] means correct vision, understanding, and perspective). «حُكماً»

7- Theoretical wisdom alone is neither beneficial nor salvific. Wisdom must be accompanied by action. «هَب لى حُكما و ألحقنى بالصّالحین»

8- Possessing wisdom and inner insight, living in the society of the righteous, and being righteous oneself, is the highest degree of felicity. «حُكماً و ألحقنى بالصّالحین»

9- Wisdom, knowledge, and insight precede action. «هب لى حُكما و ألحقنى»

10- Let us learn the best supplications from the best people. (The best people are the Prophets, and one of the greatest Prophets is Ibrahim عليه السلام, who asks Allah in his supplication for forgiveness, wisdom, a good name, and a good outcome, and in other verses, he requests good children and Muslim followers from Allah).

11- Seeking governance for the purpose of implementing Divine decrees is desirable. «هَب لى حُكما» (22)

12- Both isolation and being drawn to the unworthy are forbidden. «ألحقنى بالصّالحین»

13- We should respect the righteous predecessors. «ألحِقنى بالصّالحین» (He did not say: Let me be among the righteous, but rather: Join me with them. This is because the status of the predecessors is superior).

14- A society is valuable only if its members are righteous. «ألحِقنى بالصّالحین»

15- Man is in need of a good companion. «و ألحِقنى بالصّالحین»

22) Tafsir Safi.

Ayahs

وَٱجْعَل لِّى لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۸٤)
Confer on me a worthy repute among the posterity, (84)
وَٱجْعَلْنِى مِن وَرَثَةِ جَنَّةِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (۸٥)
and make me one of the heirs to the paradise of bliss. (85)

Key Points

In Ayah 108 of Surah As-Saffat, where the story of the sacrifice of Isma'il is mentioned, Allah says: «و تَرَكنا علیه فى الآخرین» (And We left for him [a good name] among later generations). This means We made the sacrifice during the Hajj rituals an obligatory tradition, and because of the submission of Ibrahim, We left a good name for him, and thus answered his prayer when he said: «واجعل لى لسان صدق فى الآخرین» (And grant me an honorable mention among later generations).

In Ayah 50 of Surah Maryam, following the mention of the names of Prophets Ibrahim, Ishaq (Isaac), and Ya'qub (Jacob), Allah says: «و جَعلنا لهم لسان صِدق عَلیّاً» (And We granted them a lofty, honorable mention). We established a high and good name for them. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: A good name is better than wealth and capital.(23)

In narrations, lisan sidq (honorable mention) and good legacy have been interpreted as referring to the blessed existence of Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and Ali عليه السلام.(24) Some have considered the statement of the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, who said: «أنَا دَعوة أبى ابراهیم» (I am the result of the prayer of my father Ibrahim), as evidence for this point. Therefore, the intent of lisan sidq among later generations refers to the prophets from his lineage who kept the monotheistic school of thought alive, and this wish is more fitting for the status of a prophet like Ibrahim عليه السلام.

We read in narrations: When a person dies, the record of his deeds is closed, except for someone who has left behind an ongoing charity (sadaqah jariyah), such as an endowment (waqf), or a beneficial book left behind by him, or a righteous child who prays for him.(25)

It is mentioned in narrations: Every human being has a place in Paradise and a place in Hellfire, and when he enters Hellfire, his house in Paradise is given to others, and they become the inheritors.(26)

23) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 24) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq. 25) Wasa'il, Vol. 6, p. 293. 26) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 2, p. 31.

Messages

1- We should ask Him for the permanence of the name of the school of thought and a good legacy. «واجعَل لى» (And grant me)

2- Praise is valuable only if it is sincere, not based on flattery. «لسان صدق» (honorable mention/tongue of truth)

3- Take future generations into account and consider their judgment. «لسان صِدقٍ فى الآخرین» (an honorable mention among later generations)

4- The consensus of Jews, Christians, and Muslims on the greatness of Ibrahim is an example of the acceptance of his prayer. «لسان صِدقٍ فى الآخرین»

5- Great people desire both worldly popularity and otherworldly felicity. «لسان صِدق فى الآخرین»

6- Even after death, a person benefits from the good prayers of others and the virtuous traditions He established. «لسان صِدق فى الآخرین»

7- We should not be content with short-term plans. «لسان صدق فى الآخرین»

8- The blessings of Paradise are a gift without hardship in return for our deeds. «ورثة جنّةالنّعیم» (inheritors of the Garden of Bliss), because inheritance refers to wealth acquired without toil or effort.

Ayahs

وَٱغْفِرْ لِأَبِىٓ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلضَّآلِّينَ (۸٦)
Forgive my father, for he is one of those who are astray. (86)
وَلَا تُخْزِنِى يَوْمَ يُبْعَثُونَ (۸۷)
Do not disgrace me on the day that they will be resurrected, (87)
يَوْمَ لَا يَنفَعُ مَالٌ وَلَا بَنُونَ (۸۸)
the day when neither wealth nor children will avail, (88)
إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى ٱللَّهَ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ (۸۹)
except him who comes to Allah with a sound heart,’ (89)

Key Points

It is well-known that the meaning of "father" here refers to Abraham's uncle, and according to the Quran, a believer should not pray for a disbeliever. Therefore, if Prophet Abraham prayed for his disbelieving uncle, it was because He had promised him a prayer, as he still hoped for his conversion to faith. «سَأستغفر لك ربّى»(27) However, as soon as he became certain that he would not believe, he disassociated himself from his uncle. «و ما كان استغفار ابراهیم لابیه الاّ عن مَوعدة وَعدَها ایّاه فلمّا تبیّن له أنّه عَدوٌّ للَّه تبرّأ منه»(28)

A pure soul is a soul that is free from attachment to the world and contamination by polytheism (shirk), disbelief (kufr), hypocrisy (nifaq), arrogance (takabbur), and all spiritual diseases. Since Allah says concerning the hypocrites: «فى قلوبهم مَرض» (in their hearts is a disease), therefore, the sound heart (qalb salīm) is the heart of the believer. We read in a hadith: The sound heart is a heart in which there is no polytheism (shirk).(29)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله used to recite this supplication in his prayer: «الّلهم لاتخزنى یوم القیامة» (O Lord! Do not disgrace and humiliate me on the Day of Resurrection).(30)

Question: We read in narrations that spending wealth (infaq), continuous charity (sadaqah jāriyah), and righteous children who pray for their parents are beneficial for a person's Hereafter. So, what is the relationship between the verse «لایَنفع مال و لابَنون» (Wealth and sons will not benefit) and these narrations?

Answer: The intent of this verse is wealth and children that are not aligned with the Divine path. Therefore, the world is like a key that locks a door with one movement and opens it with another. Lawful wealth in the hands of righteous people leads to proximity (to Allah), while in the hands of the unworthy, it leads to sin.

If you bear wealth for the sake of faith, "What excellent righteous wealth!" said that Messenger. What is the world? It is becoming heedless of Allah, Not garments, silver, children, or wife. Water inside the ship is the ship's destruction, Water outside the ship is its support.(31)

27) Maryam, 47. 28) Tawbah, 114. 29) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 30) Tafsir Furqan. 31) Bayan al-Sa'adah.

Messages

1- Love for and prayer concerning relatives must be accompanied by enjoining the right and criticizing their mistakes. «قال لابیه و قومه ما تعبدون ... واغفر لابى»

2- Worldly connections and arrangements are ineffective on the Day of Resurrection, and nothing is functional except a pure heart. «خَطیئتى یوم الدّین ... قلب سلیم»

3- Fear and hope must exist side by side. «اَطمع أن یَغفر لى - لاتُخزِنى یوم یبعثون»

4- A person, regardless of their stage (of life or piety), should not be heedless of the Day of Resurrection. «لاتُخزِنى...»

5- On the Day of Resurrection, others will also become aware of our faults. «لا تُخزِنى» because disgrace occurs where others become aware of the specifics of our actions and thoughts.

6- Humiliation and disgrace are among the most difficult states on the Day of Resurrection. «لا تُخزِنى» Prophet Abraham mentions avoiding disgrace among all the difficulties of the Day of Resurrection.

7- On the Day of Resurrection, contempt, humiliation, and disgrace are worse than the torment of Hellfire. «لا تُخزِنى» In another place, we read: «انّك مَن تُدخل النّار فقد اَخزیتَه»(32)

8- What is valuable in this world becomes worthless in the Hereafter. «لاینفع مال ولابنون» "Wealth" (مال) is the manifestation of material matters, and "sons" (بَنُون) is the manifestation of emotional matters; meaning that on the Day of Resurrection, wealth, children, status, fame, degrees, and so forth, will not be salvific.

32) Aal-i Imran, 192.

Ayahs

وَأُزْلِفَتِ ٱلْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (۹٠)
and paradise will be brought near for the Godwary, (90)
وَبُرِّزَتِ ٱلْجَحِيمُ لِلْغَاوِينَ (۹۱)
and hell will be brought into view for the perverse, (91)
وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ (۹۲)
and they shall be told: ‘Where is that which you used to worship (92)
مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ هَلْ يَنصُرُونَكُمْ أَوْ يَنتَصِرُونَ (۹۳)
besides Allah? Do they help you, or do they help each other?’ (93)
فَكُبْكِبُوا۟ فِيهَا هُمْ وَٱلْغَاوُۥنَ (۹٤)
Then they will be cast into it on their faces—they and the perverse, (94)
وَجُنُودُ إِبْلِيسَ أَجْمَعُونَ (۹٥)
and the hosts of Iblis all together. (95)

Key Points

«اُزلفت» means drawing near, and «بُرّزت», derived from «Burūz» (manifestation), means becoming apparent. «غاوین» (the misguided/deviants), derived from «Ghawā'», refers to evil and ignorance resulting from corrupted belief. «كُبّ» means falling face-first to the ground, and «كُبكُب» means falling face-first repeatedly. It is as if the inhabitants of Hell are continuously falling and dropping into Hell, similar to a stone falling down several steps, hitting the ground once on each step. Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq علیهما السلام said: Those who speak of justice but are not just in action are the manifestation of this Ayah: «كُبكِبوا فیها هم والغاوُون» (33) The author of Tafsir Atayab al-Bayān considers the meaning of Paradise drawing near and Hell becoming apparent to be the opening of the door of Paradise to the grave of the believer and the door of Hell to the grave of the disbeliever.

(33) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The status of the pious believer is higher than Paradise itself. Therefore, Paradise is brought near for him. «اُزلِفَت الجنّة للمتّقین» Yes, whoever kept distance from sin in this world, Paradise, with all its grandeur, will be brought forth to welcome him on the Day of Judgment.

2- Allah's promise (of reward) precedes His threat (of punishment). «اُزلفت الجنّة - بُرّزت الجحیم»

3- Seeing Hell and the means of torment is itself a form of torture for the inhabitants of Hell. «بُرّزت الجحیم»

4- Reproach is one of the spiritual torments of the Day of Judgment. «قیل لهم این ما كنتم ...»

5- Human beliefs play an important role in his destiny. «قیل لهم این ما كنتم»

6- On the Day of Judgment, questions will be asked about beliefs and thoughts. «قیل لهم این ما كنتم تعبدون»

7- The spirit of worship exists in all human beings, although in some of them it is led astray. «این ما كنتم تعبدون»

8- Both the imaginary deities and the misguided worshippers will go to Hell. «هم والغاوُون»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ وَهُمْ فِيهَا يَخْتَصِمُونَ (۹٦)
They will say, as they wrangle in it [together], (96)
تَٱللَّهِ إِن كُنَّا لَفِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۹۷)
‘By Allah, we had indeed been in manifest error (97)
إِذْ نُسَوِّيكُم بِرَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۹۸)
when we equated you with the Lord of all the worlds! (98)
وَمَآ أَضَلَّنَآ إِلَّا ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (۹۹)
No one led us astray except the guilty. (99)

Key Points

Contrary to the atmosphere of Paradise, which is entirely peace and safety, the atmosphere of Hell is always full of blame, hostility, and cursing: the cursing of followers upon corrupt leaders, the blame of sinners toward Satan, the aversion of the worshipped deities from the polytheists, and the disavowal of the criminals from one another. Numerous verses concerning these matters are found in the Quran.

Messages

1- Among the inhabitants of Hell, there is dispute, argument, and hostility. (The polytheists are constantly in conflict with their worshipped deities). «یختصمون»

2- On the Day of Judgment, unholy and non-Divine loves turn into hatred. «یختصمون» In Surah Az-Zukhruf, Ayah 67, we read: «اَلاَخلاّء یومئذٍ بعضهم لبعضٍ عدوّ الاّ المتّقین»

3- On the Day of Judgment, consciences awaken. «اِن كنّا لفى ضَلال»

4- If we place anyone or anything alongside Allah, we have committed polytheism (Shirk). «اذ نُسَوّیكم» (Saying "in the name of God and in the name of creation" is an instance of «نُسوّیكم بربّ العالمین» [we equated you with the Lord of the worlds].)

5- On the Day of Judgment, everyone throws the blame onto someone else. «وما اضلّنا الاّ المجرمون»

6- On the Day of Judgment, the polytheists complain about their leaders. «وما اضلّنا الاّ المجرمون»

Ayahs

فَمَا لَنَا مِن شَـٰفِعِينَ (۱٠٠)
Now we have no intercessors, (100)
وَلَا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍ (۱٠۱)
nor do we have any sympathetic friend. (101)
فَلَوْ أَنَّ لَنَا كَرَّةً فَنَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٠۲)
Had there been another turn for us, we would be among the faithful.’ (102)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱٠۳)
There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (103)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٠٤)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (104)

Key Points

In Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, narrations are cited stating that the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام) have sworn to intercede for their followers (Shi'a). Imam Sadiq (علیه السلام) said: The believer will intercede for his family on that Day. (34) Imam Sadiq (علیه السلام) said: By Allah, on the Day of Resurrection, we will intercede for our followers in such a way that the voices of the deviants will rise (saying): «فما لنا من شافعین و لاصَدیق حَمیم» (35) The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: On the Day of Resurrection, the Quran, the family of the believer, the Prophet, and the Ahl al-Bayt (علیهم السلام) will intercede. (36)

34) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 35) Tafsir al-Mizan. 36) Kanz al-'Ummal.

Messages

1- Intercession is certain on the Day of Resurrection, but a group, including the polytheists, will not benefit from it. «اذ نُسوّیكم بربّ العالمین... فما لنا من شافعین»

2- Friendship with sinners in this world yields no benefit in the Hereafter. «و لا صدیق حمیم»

3- Man is in need of a good friend even on the Day of Resurrection. «و لاصدیق حمیم»

4- The condition for receiving intercession is faith (Iman). «فما لنا من شافعین - فلو اَنّ لنا كَرّة فنكون من المؤمنین»
