5- The Day of Resurrection is a day of regret. «فلو اَنّ لنا كَرّة»

6- If people are unwilling and obstinate, even a great Divine sign will not be effective in guiding them. «لآیة و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

7- Mercy alongside power is a Divine attribute. «العزیز الرّحیم»

8- O Prophet! Do not worry about the deviation of the majority, for you are under Our special protection. «و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین و اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز الرّحیم»

9- The respite that Allah grants to the deviants is not due to inability or weakness, but rather due to His mercy and grace, so that perhaps they may return. «العزیز الرّحیم»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱٠٥)
The people of Noah impugned the apostles (105)
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ نُوحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۱٠٦)
when Noah, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (106)
إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (۱٠۷)
Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (107)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱٠۸)
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (108)
وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠۹)
I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (109)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱۱٠)
So be wary of Allah and obey me.’ (110)
۞ قَالُوٓا۟ أَنُؤْمِنُ لَكَ وَٱتَّبَعَكَ ٱلْأَرْذَلُونَ (۱۱۱)
They said, ‘Shall we believe in you, when it is the riffraff who follow you?’ (111)

Key Points

The name of Prophet Nuh (Noah) is mentioned 43 times in the Quran, and Allah considered Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) to be his follower (Shi'a): «و اِنّ مِن شِیعَته لابراهیم»(37). A Surah named Nuh also appears in the Quran. Allah's salutation (peace) upon Nuh is a special salutation: «سلامٌ على نوح فى العالَمین»(38), whereas Allah has also sent salutations upon Prophets Musa (Moses), Harun (Aaron), and Ibrahim علیهم السلام, but without the phrase «فى العالَمین» (in all the worlds). Familiarity with the history of the Prophets makes man devoted to them and revives the spirit of patience and steadfastness within him.

(37) Saffat, 83. (38) Saffat, 79.

Messages

1- All Prophets share a single goal; therefore, denying one Prophet is equivalent to denying all Prophets. «كَذّبت قومُ نوحٍ المرسلین» (The people of Nuh only denied Prophet Nuh, but the Quran says: «المرسلین», meaning all the Prophets).

2- Tribes and nations are known by the names of their leaders and personalities. «قوم نوح» (The people of Nuh).

3- There were numerous Prophets before Nuh. «المرسلین»

4- In propagating the religion, utilize emotions. «اَخوهم» (Their brother).

5- The morals and conduct of the Prophets toward the people of their time were brotherly. «اَخوهم»

6- Knowledge of the people's culture, language, customs, traditions, and their strengths and weaknesses is essential for the preacher. «اَخوهم» One who is the people's brother is aware of their thoughts, customs, and weaknesses and strengths.

7- One of the duties of religious brotherhood is guiding other brothers. «اَخوهم ... اَلا تَتّقون»

8- Awaken consciences through questioning. «اَلا تَتّقون» (Will you not fear God?)

9- Accepting the invitation of the Prophets and obeying them requires a spirit of piety (Taqwa). «اَلا تَتّقون»

10- Prophethood is for the benefit of humanity and for the salvation of mankind. «اِنّى لكم رسولٌ أمین» (Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger).

11- Being trustworthy, having a good track record, and being compassionate are conditions for the effectiveness of speech. «لكم رسول أمین»

12- The main condition for the messenger and carrier of the message is trustworthiness. «رسول أمین»

13- Stating one's own merits is necessary in certain situations. «انّى لكم رسول أمین»

14- Religiousness is inseparable from obedience to divine leadership. «فاتّقوا اللّه و اَطیعون» (So fear Allah and obey me). Yes, the manifestation of piety is in following the Prophets.

15- Calling people to oneself is permissible for the purpose of reviving Allah's command. «اَطیعون» (Obey me) appears alongside «فاتّقوا اللّه» (Fear Allah).

16- The religious preacher should not expect material reward from the people or ask anything from them. (Sincerity is a condition for the effectiveness of speech). «ما أسئلكم علیه من أجر» (I ask you for no payment for it).

17- One who relies upon Allah can be independent of people. «ما أسئلكم... اِن أجرى الاّ على ربّ العالمین» (I ask you... My reward is only from the Lord of the worlds).

18- Allah has made the reward of religious preachers incumbent upon Himself. «اِن أجرى الاّعلى ربّ...»

19- The religious leader should not be in a position to be accused of worldliness. «و ما أسئلكم علیه من أجر»

20- Close the doors to excuses and do not perform spiritual deeds with the intention of material reward or wages. «ما أسئلكم علیه من أجر» We also read in Surah Al-Qalam, Ayah 46: «أم تَسئلهم أجراً فهم من مَغرَم مُثقَلون» (Or do you ask them for a payment, so they are burdened with debt?).

21- Faith in Allah and divine piety (Taqwa) are the prerequisites for faith in the Messenger and obedience to him. «فاتّقوا اللّه و اَطیعون»

22- Repetition is necessary on the path of training and perfection. (In these few verses, the command for piety has been issued several times). «فاتّقوا اللّه...»

23- Aristocracy (elitism) can be an obstacle to believing, because believers are considered lowlifes and contemptible by the arrogant. «و اتّبعك الاَرذَلون» (And only the lowest followed you).

24- Most of the companions of the Prophets were the poor and the unknown. «و اتّبعك الارذَلون»

25- The word of truth and the school of truth should not be belittled because of its unknown followers. «و اتّبعك الارذَلون»

26- The teachings of the Prophets eliminate false social discriminations. «و اتّبعك الارذَلون»

Ayahs

قَالَ وَمَا عِلْمِى بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۱۲)
He said, ‘What do I know as to what they used to do? (112)
إِنْ حِسَابُهُمْ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّى ۖ لَوْ تَشْعُرُونَ (۱۱۳)
Their reckoning is only with my Lord, should you be aware. (113)

Key Points

In Islam, we are commanded by the apparent (outward actions), and if someone embraces Islam, we do not have the right to say, "You do not have faith in your heart." «و لاتَقولوا لمَن ألقى الیكم السّلام لَستَ مؤمنا»(39) In another Ayah, the Quran instructs the Prophet: Do not drive away those who call upon Him day and night seeking closeness to Him. Their reckoning is with Allah. Neither are you responsible for their reckoning, nor are they responsible for yours, and if you drive them away, you would be among the wrongdoers.(40)

39) Nisa, 94. 40) An'am, 52.

Messages

1- Prophets are not aware of the past deeds of individuals without divine will. «وماعلمى»

2- Saying, "I do not know," is not a fault. «وماعلمى»

3- Allah is the Knower of His servants and He is the Reckoner; it is not our business to reject or accept (them). «و ما عِلمى...»

4- Spying and fault-finding regarding the private lives of individuals is forbidden. «وما عِلمى...»

5- When judging others, look at their sincere faith and righteous deeds today, and do not hold their past against them. «بما كانوا یعملون»

6- Bad records do not prevent people from change, transformation, and repentance. «كانوا یعملون»

7- The duty of the Prophets is to reform people and invite them to Monotheism (Tawhid). They are not responsible for the reckoning of individuals. «اِن حسابهم الاّ على اللّه»

Ayahs

وَمَآ أَنَا۠ بِطَارِدِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۱٤)
I will not drive away the faithful. (114)
إِنْ أَنَا۠ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۱۱٥)
I am just a manifest warner.’ (115)
قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَـٰنُوحُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَرْجُومِينَ (۱۱٦)
They said, ‘Noah, if you do not desist, you will certainly be stoned [to death].’ (116)
قَالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِى كَذَّبُونِ (۱۱۷)
He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed my people have impugned me. (117)
فَٱفْتَحْ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَهُمْ فَتْحًا وَنَجِّنِى وَمَن مَّعِىَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۱۸)
So judge conclusively between me and them, and deliver me and the faithful who are with me.’ (118)

Key Points

The arrogant (Mustakbirūn) expected even the prophets to expel the anonymous, faithful poor, but all the prophets rejected this suggestion. Allah commands the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in Ayah 28 of Surah Al-Kahf: «وَ اصبر نفسك مع الّذین یَدعون ربّهم بالغَداة والعَشىّ یُریدون وَجهَه ولاتَعدُ عیناك عنهم تُرید زینَة الحَیاة الدّنیا و لاتُطِع مَن أغفَلنا قَلبه عن ذكرنا واتّبَعَ هَواه و كان أمرُه فُرُطا» (And keep yourself patient with those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, desiring His Face. Let not your eyes overlook them, desiring the adornments of the worldly life, and do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance and who follows his own desire, and whose affair has been excessive.) Keep yourself with the believers who sincerely call upon God morning and evening, and do not overlook them for the sake of attaining the adornments of the world, and do not follow the heedless, the pleasure-seekers, and the transgressors. The phrase «مِنَ المرجومین» (of those who are stoned) instead of «لَنَرجُمنّك» (we will surely stone you) indicates that people had been stoned even before Prophet Noah (عليه السلام).

Messages

  1. Deal decisively with the inappropriate suggestions and expectations of the arrogant. (Expelling the faithful poor is not permissible, even if it means gaining the faith of another group.) «وما اَنَا بطارد...»
  2. The criterion for value is faith, not social or economic status. «وما اَنا بطارد...»
  3. Attraction (Jādhīyah) must be at the highest level, but repulsion (Dāfi'ah) is only necessary when required. «و ما اَنَا بطارد...»
  4. The fear, warning, and admonition (Inthār) of the prophets outweigh their glad tidings (Bishārah). The phrase «اِن اَنا الاّ نذیر» (I am only a warner) appears in the Quran, but the phrase «ان انا الا بشیر» (I am only a bearer of glad tidings) does not.
  5. Warning the heedless about dangers is the best method of guidance. «اِن اَنا الاّ نذیر»
  6. Threat is one of the most important weapons used by the opponents of the prophets. «من المرجومین»
  7. Stoning is one of the oldest forms of torture. «من المرجومین»
  8. In the face of enemy threats, we should seek refuge in Allah. «قال ربّ»
  9. The lament of Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) was due to the rejection of the message (Maktab), not the threat of stoning. «رِبّ انّ قومى كذّبون» (My Lord, indeed my people have denied me) and He did not say: «یرجمون» (they are stoning me).
  10. The violence of the unworthy should not diminish your paternal and fraternal affections. «اِنّ قومى» (Indeed, my people)
  11. Allah is the Solver of difficulties. «فافتح» (So judge)
  12. First pray for the resolution of the problems of the people and the community, then for your own salvation. «فافتَح - نَجّنى» (So judge - save me)
  13. Despite threats and repression, a group of people believed in the prophets. «ومَن معى من المؤمنین» (and those with me of the believers)
  14. What is important is practical and ideological companionship, not physical or superficial presence. «ومَن مَعىَ من المؤمنین»
  15. Believers are included in the prayers of the prophets. «نجّنى و مَن معِىَ من المؤمنین» (save me and those with me of the believers)
  16. Faith alone is not enough; following and companionship are necessary. «و مَن مَعىَ» (and those with me), just as the Quran states in other instances: «والّذین آمنوا معه» (and those who believed with him) (41)

(41) Hud, 58, 66, and 94.

Ayahs

فَأَنجَيْنَـٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ ٱلْمَشْحُونِ (۱۱۹)
Thereupon We delivered him and those who were with him in the laden ark. (119)
ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا بَعْدُ ٱلْبَاقِينَ (۱۲٠)
Then We drowned the rest. (120)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۲۱)
There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (121)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱۲۲)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (122)

Key Points

The meaning of the ship being heavily laden (الفُلك المَشحون) is that Noah (عليه السلام) had placed everything needed inside the ship. Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) said: "الفلك المشحون" is a fully equipped ship ready for movement. And Imam Ali (عليه السلام) said: Ninety places for livestock had been built in that ship.(42)

42) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The prayers of the prophets and the oppressed are answered. «فانجَیناه»

2- Believers are protected under the shadow of the prophets. «فانجَیناه و مَن معه»

3- Separation from the prophets leads to destruction. «ثمّ اَغرقنا بعدُ الباقین»

4- The story of the salvation of Noah and his companions and the drowning of the disbelievers and opponents is a manifestation of Allah’s tradition (Sunnah) in aiding the truth and suppressing falsehood. «اِنّ فى ذلك لآیة»

5- If He grants respite to the disbelievers, it is due to His mercy, not His inability. «العزیز الرّحیم»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ عَادٌ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۲۳)
[The people of] ‘Ad impugned the apostles, (123)
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ هُودٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۱۲٤)
when Hud, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (124)
إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (۱۲٥)
Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (125)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱۲٦)
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (126)
وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۲۷)
I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (127)

Key Points

'Ād is the name of the great ancestor of the people of Prophet Hūd, and that nation became famous by the name of their ancestor. The people of 'Ād were a tribe of Arabs who lived in the pleasant climate of Yemen, in a region called 'Al-Ahqāf' near the sea. Their country was prosperous, the people were urban dwellers, and their Prophet was Hūd. Their name is repeated 24 times in the Holy Qur'an. Furthermore, in the Qur'an, we have a Surah named Al-Ahqāf and a Surah named Hūd.

Messages

1- Since the goal and content of the call of all Prophets are the same, denying one Prophet is equivalent to denying all Prophets. «كذّبت عادٌالمرسلین»

2- The call of the Prophets was brotherly. «اَخوهم» (The word «اَبوهم» (their father) was not even used, in order to express the depth of sincerity, humility, and truthfulness).

3- Piety (Taqwa) is the headline of the Prophets' call. «اَلا تَتّقون»

4- Calling to piety is a prerequisite for brotherhood. «اَخوهم هود اَلا تَتّقون»

5- The mission of the Prophets is for the growth of the people, not for domination over them. «لكم»

6- The Prophets did not speak on their own accord; everything they said was a Divine message. «رسول أمین»

7- The tone of all Prophets, their method of propagation, the content of their messages, and their ideological line are shared. (The repetition of the verses is evidence of this statement).

8- Divine piety paves the way for obedience to leadership. «فاتّقوا اللّه و اَطیعون» Following Divine leaders is the manifestation of Divine piety.

9- Sincerity and lack of expectation are the secrets to success. «و ما أسئلكم»

10- Sincerity and reliance (Tawakkul) are prerequisites for propagation. «اِن أجرى ...»

11- He who manages existence will also manage my life. «اِن أجرى الا على ربّ العالمین»

12- The system of existence is composed of various factors, all of which are under one management and are in a state of growth. «ربّ العالمین»

Ayahs

أَتَبْنُونَ بِكُلِّ رِيعٍ ءَايَةً تَعْبَثُونَ (۱۲۸)
Do you build futile a sign on every prominence? (128)
وَتَتَّخِذُونَ مَصَانِعَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَخْلُدُونَ (۱۲۹)
You set up structures as if you will be immortal, (129)
وَإِذَا بَطَشْتُم بَطَشْتُمْ جَبَّارِينَ (۱۳٠)
and when you seize [someone for punishment], you seize [him] like tyrants. (130)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱۳۱)
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (131)

Key Points

«Rī‘» means the slope of a mountain or hill. «Maṣāni‘» refers to a strong and tall fortress or palace. And the word «baṭsh» means seizing with force, dominance, and power. In these verses, the Quran does not criticize the principle of building structures on mountain slopes, constructing fortresses, or possessing power. What is criticized are the unholy goals («ta‘bathūn»), the vain fantasies («la‘allakum takhludūn»), and the transgression of moderation («jabbārīn»). We read in narrations: One of the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله had built a dome (qubba) on the roof of his house. The Prophet was displeased upon seeing it. When the owner saw this, he destroyed the dome. When the Messenger of Allah did not see the dome again, He asked: What happened to the dome? They said: As soon as its owner realized you were displeased, he destroyed it. The Prophet said: «Inn kullu binā’in wabālun ‘alā ṣāḥibihi illā mā lā budda minhu» (Every building that is not necessary will be a burden [wabāl] upon its owner on the Day of Judgment.) (43)

43) Tafsir al-Mizan and Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The history of tall structures dates back to the time of Hūd. «A-tabnūna bi-kulli rī‘in āyah»

2- Greed does not abandon man. «bi-kulli rī‘in»

3- What appears glorious in the eyes of the worldly is contemptible in the sight of Allah’s saints, and they rebuke people for it. «A-tabnūna... ta‘bathūn»

4- False civilization and luxury are factors of arrogance and obstacles to accepting the truth. «A-tabnūna»

5- The work of the prophets begins with criticizing the existing chaotic situation and awakening consciences through questioning. «A-tabnūna bi-kulli rī‘in...»

6- The actions of oppressors are either for self-promotion («āyah»), or for their own survival («takhludūn»), or for the exploitation and colonization of people («jabbārīn»).

7- In every action, one must have a lofty goal. «A-tabnūna... ta‘bathūn»

8- Wealth, capital, and tall buildings in the hands of the unworthy are for the satisfaction of whims and desires. «Ta‘bathūn»

9- The Quran criticizes the spirit of palace dwelling and hedonism, not the principle of construction itself. «Ta‘bathūn» (Production, innovation, art, and the utilization of resources and human power, etc., must be directed toward genuine need, not futility.)

10- Palaces and mansions do not make life eternal. «Tattakhidhūna maṣāni‘a la‘allakum takhludūn»

11- The afflictions of construction are four things:

A: Greed and extravagance. «bi-kulli rī‘in»

B: Boasting and showing off. «āyah»

C: Capriciousness/Frivolity. «ta‘bathūn»

D: Worldliness and heedlessness. «la‘allakum takhludūn»

12- Penal laws must be just. (The Quran criticizes oppressive punishments.) «baṭashtum jabbārīn»

13- Piety requires a focal point, a leader, and a role model. «Fa-ittaqū Allāha wa aṭī‘ūn»

14- Piety is both inner reverence and practical obedience. «Fa-ittaqū Allāha wa aṭī‘ūn»

15- Lack of piety is the cause of all corruption. After criticizing the thoughts and actions of the disbelievers, Prophet Hūd said: «Fa-ittaqū Allāha...»

Ayahs

وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَمَدَّكُم بِمَا تَعْلَمُونَ (۱۳۲)
Be wary of Him who has provided you with whatever you know, (132)
أَمَدَّكُم بِأَنْعَـٰمٍ وَبَنِينَ (۱۳۳)
and aided you with sons and with cattle, (133)
وَجَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (۱۳٤)
gardens and springs. (134)
إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱۳٥)
Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.’ (135)

Key Points

The phrase «فاتّقوا اللّه» relates both to Prophethood: «واَطیعون» and to the Resurrection (Ma'ad): «اِنّى أخاف علیكم عذاب یوم عظیم». The intent of the Great Day (یوم عظیم) here is the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), although in the story of Prophet Shu'ayb, the worldly punishment was also called the Great Day. «فاَخذَهم عذاب یوم الظّلة اِنّه كان عذاب یوم عظیم» The punishment of the day of the fiery cloud seized them, and that was the punishment of a Great Day.(44)

44) Surah Ash-Shu'ara, 189.

Messages

1- Piety (Taqwa) is the best way to thank Allah. «واتّقوا الّذى اَمدّكم»

2- Everyone knows for themselves how much they have been favored by Allah. «اَمدّكم بماتعلمون»

3- The palaces and buildings of the people of 'Ad were accompanied by livestock, gardens, and springs. «بكلّ ریع آیة... انعام... جنّات و عیون»

4- If there is no readiness or capacity, the admonition of the prophets will have no effect. «سواء علینا»

Ayahs

قَالُوا۟ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْنَآ أَوَعَظْتَ أَمْ لَمْ تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْوَٰعِظِينَ (۱۳٦)
They said, ‘It is the same to us whether you exhort us or not. (136)
إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّا خُلُقُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۳۷)
These are nothing but the traditions of the ancients, (137)
وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ (۱۳۸)
and we will not be punished.’ (138)
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَهْلَكْنَـٰهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۳۹)
So they impugned him, whereupon We destroyed them. There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (139)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٤٠)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (140)

Key Points

Regarding what the word "هذا" (this) refers to, there are several possibilities: A: This claim of prophethood by you is nothing but an imitation of previous claimants to prophethood. B: These tall houses on the hills and our other actions are the way of our ancestors. C: This denial and idol worship of ours is the same denial and idol worship of our ancestors. The Quran has criticized the majority of people repeatedly. In this Surah, too, the verse "و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین" (and most of them were not believers) is mentioned several times at the end of the narration of the history of the prophets.

Messages

1- The history and behavior of ancestors are not proof of truthfulness. «اِن هذا الاّ خُلُق الاوّلین» (if the intent of "هذا" is the behavior of the polytheists)

2- Improper imitation and fanaticism prevent the acceptance of advice. «سواء علینا أ وَعَظتَ... اِن هذا...»

3- The deviation of ancestors paves the way for the deviation of subsequent generations. «اِن هذا الاّ خُلُق الاوّلین»

4- Sometimes idol worship and any other behavior become part of the essence of man. (The word "خُلق" [khuluq] means a fixed disposition/nature)

5- Idolaters justified their deviation by citing the way of their ancestors and absolved themselves of responsibility. «و ما نحن بمُعذّبین»

6- Man's destiny is in his own hands. «فكذّبوه فاهلكناهم»

7- Take a lesson from the denial and destruction of others. «لآیة»

8- Do not let the majority deceive you. Do not seek the truth in the size of the population; quantity is not the criterion for recognizing truth and falsehood. «و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱٤۱)
[The people of] Thamud impugned the apostles (141)
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ صَـٰلِحٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۱٤۲)
when Salih, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (142)
إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (۱٤۳)
Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (143)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱٤٤)
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (144)
وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٤٥)
I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (145)

Key Points

In this Surah, the stories of several Prophets are narrated, but the beginning of their narratives starts with the word kadhdhabat (they denied) (45). This is because the crucial point in these stories is the issue of the denial of the Prophets by past nations and their subsequent destruction. Indeed, in writing and speaking, important matters must be placed at the forefront. When recounting history, attention must be paid to significant points, not to issues whose knowledge or ignorance yields no benefit or harm. In these stories, nothing is mentioned about the number of people, the name of the place, the date of the incident, or other details, because what serves as a lesson (for reflection) is the demonstration of the manifestations of truth and falsehood and the declaration of the victory of truth over falsehood.

45) The story of Noah, Ayah 105; the story of 'Ad, Ayah 123; the story of Salih, Ayah 141; the story of Lot, Ayah 160; and the story of Shu'ayb, Ayah 176.

Messages

1- The method of invitation (Da'wah) of all the Prophets is the same. In the stories of Noah, Hud, Salih, Lot, and Shu'ayb, all of them had one message and one slogan:

All of them said: «اَلا تتّقون» (Will you not fear Allah?)

All of them said: «فاتّقوا اللّه واَطیعون» (So fear Allah and obey me.)

All of them said: «اِنّى لكم رسول أمین» (Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.)

All of them said: «ما أسئلكم علیه من أجر» (And I ask you not for any reward for it.)

And the summary of all their messages consisted of several things: brotherhood, piety (taqwa), following the leader, lack of expectation (of reward), reliance upon Allah, and assuring the people that "we are for your benefit and trustworthy among you." (The messages of these few verses are also similar to the messages of the previous verses concerning the other Prophets mentioned in this same Surah.)

Ayahs

أَتُتْرَكُونَ فِى مَا هَـٰهُنَآ ءَامِنِينَ (۱٤٦)
Will you be left secure in that which is here (146)
فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (۱٤۷)
—amid gardens and springs, (147)
وَزُرُوعٍ وَنَخْلٍ طَلْعُهَا هَضِيمٌ (۱٤۸)
farms and date palms with dainty spathes (148)
وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ ٱلْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا فَـٰرِهِينَ (۱٤۹)
—while you skillfully hew out houses from the mountains? (149)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱٥٠)
So be wary of Allah and obey me, (150)
وَلَا تُطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۱٥۱)
and do not obey the dictates of the transgressors, (151)
ٱلَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا يُصْلِحُونَ (۱٥۲)
who cause corruption in the land and do not bring about reform.’ (152)

Key Points

If the people of the world were to act solely upon this phrase, "لا تُطیعوا امر المسرفین" (Do not obey the command of the extravagant), all tyrannical, extravagant governments would fall.

The people of Thamūd had good agriculture, "فى جنّات و عُیون و زُروع" (in gardens and springs and cultivated fields), civilization and house-building, "من الجبال بیوتاً" (houses from the mountains), and also prosperity and security. "فارِهین... آمِنین" (prosperous... secure).

The word "طَلع" (ṭal‘) refers to the date blossom that appears before the fruit, and sometimes it is also used for the first or early dates.

"هَضیم" (haḍīm) means a delicate and clustered blossom. This means the date blossoms were piled upon one another due to their abundance, high yield, and freshness.

Although the word "جنّات" (gardens) includes date palms, the name of the date palm was mentioned separately, which perhaps indicates the importance of dates among the people of Thamūd.

The word "فارهین" (fārihīn) could derive from the word "farah," meaning joy, in which case "بیوتا فارهین" means they were exultant in their houses. It could also derive from the root "farāhat," meaning skill, in which case "بیوتاً فارهین" means they skillfully carved houses out of the mountains.

Messages

1- Do not be deceived by the luxurious life of this world. "أتُتركون... آمِنین" (Will you be left... secure?). Today's prosperity is not a sign of Allah's satisfaction with you. (Material blessings are not lasting).

2- Attention to the impermanence of the world is a means to piety (taqwa). "أتُتركون... فاتّقوا اللّه" (Will you be left... so fear Allah).

3- The prerequisite for piety is following the Prophets and avoiding obedience to the extravagant. "فاتّقوا اللّه و اَطیعون و لا تُطیعوا أمر المسرفین" (So fear Allah and obey me, and do not obey the command of the extravagant).

4- Security, economy, and prosperity, if not accompanied by following an infallible leader, will become a ground for corruption. "آمِنین، جنّات، عیون، زُروع، بُیوتاً، فاتّقوا اللّه و اَطیعون" (secure, gardens, springs, cultivated fields, houses, so fear Allah and obey me).

5- Man is not left unrestrained regarding blessings; rather, he has responsibility. "جنّات، عیون، زُروع، بُیوتاً، لاتُطیعوا أمر المسرفین" (gardens, springs, cultivated fields, houses, do not obey the command of the extravagant).

6- The extravagant person is not qualified to lead society. "لاتُطیعوا أمر المسرفین" (Do not obey the command of the extravagant).

7- The Prophets were sensitive regarding the consumerism of the people. "لاتُطیعوا أمر المسرفین" (Do not obey the command of the extravagant).

8- Do not submit to extravagant capitalists. "لاتُطیعوا أمر المسرفین" (Do not obey the command of the extravagant).

9- Tawallī (loving/following) and Tabarrī (disassociating/rejecting) must go together. "اطیعون - لا تطیعوا" (Obey me - Do not obey).

10- Extravagance is corruption, and the extravagant person is a corrupter. "المسرفین الّذین یُفسِدون" (The extravagant ones who cause corruption).

11- Do not expect reform from the heedless affluent, as reform is only possible under the guidance of the school of the Prophets. "و لا یُصلِحون" (and they do not reform).

12- The extravagant justify their actions and consider themselves righteous. "لایُصلِحون" (they do not reform).

Ayahs

قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسَحَّرِينَ (۱٥۳)
They said, ‘Indeed you are one of the bewitched. (153)
مَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا فَأْتِ بِـَٔايَةٍ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۱٥٤)
You are just a human being like us. So bring us a sign, should you be truthful.’ (154)

Messages

1- They leveled two accusations against the prophets: being a sorcerer ("ساحر") and being bewitched ("المسحّرین").

2- Worse than disobedience is justifying it. In response to the command "اَطیعون" (Obey me), they justified their disobedience by saying: You are bewitched and not worthy of being followed. ("المسحّرین")

3- Prophethood and miracle are inseparable. "فأتِ بآیة" (Bring forth a sign.) (The miracle is the sign of the prophets' truthfulness.)

Ayahs

قَالَ هَـٰذِهِۦ نَاقَةٌ لَّهَا شِرْبٌ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍ مَّعْلُومٍ (۱٥٥)
He said, ‘This is a she-camel; she shall drink and you shall drink on known days. (155)
وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوٓءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱٥٦)
Do not cause her any harm, for then you shall be seized by the punishment of a terrible day.’ (156)
فَعَقَرُوهَا فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ نَـٰدِمِينَ (۱٥۷)
But they hamstrung her, whereupon they became regretful. (157)

Key Points

The hand of Allah's power is open in presenting miracles; from splitting the moon («اِنشقّ القَمر»), to splitting the water («فانفلق»), to splitting the mountain and bringing forth the camel—it is all the same to Him. Although only one person killed the she-camel (Nāqah) of Salih, the Quran says: «عقروها» (they slaughtered her), meaning a group slaughtered her, because that group was pleased with the action of that one person. Just as Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «انّما عقر ناقة ثمود رجل واحد فعمّهم اللّه بالعذاب لمّا عمّوه بالرضا»(46)

46) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 201.

Messages

1- The miracle must be so clear that all people, regardless of their level, can comprehend its miraculous nature. «هذه ناقة»

2- Insulting sacred things carries a great penalty. «لاتَمَسّوها بسوء فیأخذكم» Allah attributed this camel to Himself in Surah Al-A'raf: «ناقة اللّه»(47)

3- The fair distribution of water resources must take place according to God's command. «لها شربٌ ولكم»

4- A camel that bears a divine color and sign takes precedence over ordinary humans. «لها شِرب و لكم...»

5- The rebellious human does not even show mercy to a harmless, milk-giving animal. «فعَقَروها»

6- We must take the warnings of the prophets seriously. «لاتمسّوها بسوء - فعَقَروها فاصبحوا نادمین»

7- Whoever is pleased with the action of another is a partner in the reward or the sin of that action. «فعقروها», even though only one person killed the she-camel.

8- Not every regret and repentance is accepted. (Repentance and regret upon seeing the punishment are ineffective). «نادمین - فأخذهم العذاب»

47) Al-A'raf, 73.

Ayahs

فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱٥۸)
So the punishment seized them. There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (158)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱٥۹)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (159)

Messages

1- Insulting divine sanctities is so serious that a group is destroyed merely for killing an animal. «فعَقَروها ... فأخذهم العذاب»

2- Both His graces and His wrath and punishment are signs for reflection. «لآیةً»

3- Man can remain obstinate against the truth and refuse to believe, even after seeing the greatest divine signs. «لآیة و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

4- Sending the prophets, miracles, and inflicting wrath upon stubborn disbelievers are among the affairs of His Lordship. «ربّك»

5- The faith and disbelief of people have no effect on Allah. «اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز»

6- Allah's wrath is due to our own actions; otherwise, He is Merciful. «لهوالعزیزالرحیم»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱٦٠)
The people of Lot impugned the apostles (160)
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ لُوطٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۱٦۱)
when Lot, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (161)
إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (۱٦۲)
Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (162)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱٦۳)
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (163)
وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٦٤)
I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (164)

Key Points

Prophet Lot and Prophet Abraham were contemporaries, but in this Surah, the distance between the story of Abraham and the story of Lot is about one hundred verses. This is because, in the Quranic narratives, the lessons (admonitions) are important, not the chronological arrangement and historical sequence.

Messages

1- Place the main subjects at the beginning of your discourse. «كذّبت ثمود ...» (At the beginning of the history of all the prophets in this Surah, the word «كذّبت» (denied/rejected) is mentioned. This is because the purpose of relating the history of those great figures in this Surah is to describe the denial by the stubborn people and their divine punishment.)

2- Denying one prophet is equivalent to denying all the prophets. «كذّبت... المرسلین» (Although every nation denied its own prophet, since the prophets share a single goal and program, denying one of them is equivalent to denying all of them.)

3- The relationship between the heavenly leader and the people is one of brotherhood. «اخوهم»

4- Whoever lacks inner piety (Taqwa) is vulnerable in every respect and accepts no truth. «الا تتّقون»

5- Heavenly leaders must approach the people and present themselves and their goals to them. «الا تتّقون انّى لكم ...»

6- Piety (Taqwa) is the foundation and context for accepting the truth and obeying the prophets. «فاتّقوا اللّه و اطیعون»

7- Religiousness is incomplete without obedience to the leadership. «فاتّقوا اللّه و اطیعون»

8- If we do not covet people's wealth, our words will have a greater impact. «ما اسئلكم»

9- Only he whose reliance (Tawakkul) upon Allah is great can be independent of the people. «الا على ربّ العالمین»

Ayahs

أَتَأْتُونَ ٱلذُّكْرَانَ مِنَ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٦٥)
What! Of all people do you come to males, (165)
وَتَذَرُونَ مَا خَلَقَ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّنْ أَزْوَٰجِكُم ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ عَادُونَ (۱٦٦)
abandoning your wives your Lord has created for you? Indeed, you are a transgressing lot.’ (166)
قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَـٰلُوطُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُخْرَجِينَ (۱٦۷)
They said, ‘Lot, if you do not desist, you will surely be banished.’ (167)

Key Points

In these verses, the people of Lot (قوم لوط) are introduced as transgressors: «بل انتم قوم عادون» (Rather, you are a transgressing people). However, other expressions have also been used about them in other verses, including: «بل انتم قوم مسرفون» (Rather, you are a people who commit excess) (48), «قوم سوء فاسقین» (A people of evil, disobedient) (49), and «بل انتم قوم تجهلون» (Rather, you are a people who act ignorantly) (50).

The punishment for someone who commits sodomy (liwāṭ) is execution. This act is so vile that even if someone engages in sexual intercourse with an animal, the meat of that animal becomes forbidden (ḥarām), and the animal must be slaughtered, and then its carcass must be burned.

Homosexuality is only observed among human beings. In the universe, no other creature is homosexual.

48) Al-A'raf, 81.

49) Al-Anbiya, 74.

50) An-Naml, 55.

Messages

1- In enjoining the good and forbidding the evil (Nahy ‘anil Munkar), one must recognize the prevalent evils in every group and time and insist on restraining people from them. «أتأتون الذُكران»

2- In forbidding the evil, present the known (legitimate) paths. «أتأتون - و تذرون... ازواجكم»

3- Abandoning one's spouse and ignoring her needs is forbidden. «تَذَرون... ازواجكم»

4- Allah has established a natural and innate path for satisfying human instincts. «خلق لكم»

5- Heavenly religions encourage humans toward marriage. «خلق لكم ربّكم من ازواجكم»

6- Marriage is a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and is beneficial for humanity. «خلق لكم ربّكم»

7- Sexual deviations are transgression and rebellion, and homosexuality (same-sex attraction) and sodomy (liwāṭ) are transgressions against the boundaries of values. «عادون»

8- To eliminate evil, repetition of forbidding the evil and steadfastness are necessary. «لئن لم تَنتَه»

9- In forbidding the evil, prepare yourself for the threats of others. «لتكوننّ من المخرجین»

10- Exile was the method used by the oppressors among the people of Lot. «من المخرجین»

Ayahs

قَالَ إِنِّى لِعَمَلِكُم مِّنَ ٱلْقَالِينَ (۱٦۸)
He said, ‘Indeed I detest your conduct.’ (168)
رَبِّ نَجِّنِى وَأَهْلِى مِمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٦۹)
‘My Lord! Deliver me and my family from what they do.’ (169)
فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَأَهْلَهُۥٓ أَجْمَعِينَ (۱۷٠)
So We delivered him and all his family, (170)
إِلَّا عَجُوزًا فِى ٱلْغَـٰبِرِينَ (۱۷۱)
except an old woman who remained behind. (171)
ثُمَّ دَمَّرْنَا ٱلْـَٔاخَرِينَ (۱۷۲)
Then We destroyed [all] the rest, (172)
وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًا ۖ فَسَآءَ مَطَرُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (۱۷۳)
and rained down upon them a rain [of stones]. Evil was the rain of those who were warned! (173)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۷٤)
There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (174)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱۷٥)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (175)

Key Points

The word qālīn (قالین) means the expression of severe concern and opposition that comes from the depth of the soul.

Messages

1- Oppose the evil deed, not the person who commits the transgression. «لعمَلِكم من القالین»

2- The Prophets were not afraid of threats and spoke their minds. «من القالین» In response to the threat of expulsion and exile, Lot said: I am an enemy of your deed.

3- Detestation of evil deeds must be both verbal and practical. «قال اِنّى لعملكم - ربّ نجّنى» Yes, mere concern is not enough; one must think about liberation and salvation from the center of corruption.

4- A polluted environment is intolerable for the men of God; if they despair of reforming the environment, they at least save themselves from it. «نجّنى»

5- The leaders of society must be concerned about their family and followers. «نجّنى و اهلى»

6- The prayers of the Prophets are answered. «ربّ نجّنى - فنجّیناه»

7- A woman is not dependent on her husband in choosing her path. «الاّ عجوزاً» (Lot's wife had chosen the path of deviation.)

8- In the divine religions, all laws and values are based on criteria, not relationships. «الاّ عجوزًا» (Even the Prophet's wife, if she deviates, will be destroyed, because the standard and measure is disbelief and faith, not relationships and affiliations.)

9- Separate the account of the associates and relatives of prominent figures from the account of the figures themselves. «الاّ عجوزًا» (It is possible for a person to be a Prophet and Messenger of Allah, yet his relatives may not be on the path of truth. Lot's wife was in the Prophet's house, but she held deviant thoughts.)

10- The punishment for sin is the same for everyone; Lot's wife was among those destroyed and had no special privilege. «فى الغابرین»

11- The punishment for those who divert the path of marriage toward sodomy is that the rain of mercy turns into the rain of torment and destroys them. «فساء مَطر المنذرین»

12- Allah warns and completes the argument (proof) before inflicting punishment. «المنذرین»

13- As soon as a society is devoid of righteous individuals, the ground for divine wrath is prepared. «نجّیناه و اهله... ثمّ دمّرنا»

14- Nature is under the control of Divine power. (The same power that sends down rain from the sky can send down stones.) «فامطرنا علیهم مطراً...»

15- History is a source of lesson. «اِنّ فى ذلك لایةً»

16- Repetition is necessary in education. (In this Surah, after recounting the story of each of the Prophets, the phrase «اِنّ فى ذلك لآیة» (Indeed, in that is a sign) is mentioned.)

17- Most people are not receptive to advice. «و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین»

18- Power coupled with mercy is valuable. «العزیز الرّحیم»

Ayahs

كَذَّبَ أَصْحَـٰبُ لْـَٔيْكَةِ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (۱۷٦)
The inhabitants of Aykah impugned the apostles, (176)
إِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ شُعَيْبٌ أَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۱۷۷)
when Shu‘ayb said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (177)
إِنِّى لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (۱۷۸)
Indeed I am a trusted apostle [sent] to you. (178)
فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (۱۷۹)
So be wary of Allah and obey me. (179)
وَمَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَىٰ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۸٠)
I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (180)

Key Points

| "Al-Aykah" (الأيكة) means a thicket or a forest. Some consider the "Companions of Al-Aykah" (who are mentioned four times in the Quran) to be the same as the Companions of Madyan, while others consider them to be a people who lived in an area near Madyan. Both groups were addressed by Prophet Shu'ayb (عليه السلام). In this Surah, this is the seventh prophet whose story is narrated. In all these stories, the phrase "قال لهم اَخوهم" (their brother said to them) was used, but in the case of Shu'ayb, the word "اَخوهم" (their brother) is not mentioned. Perhaps the reason for this is that Shu'ayb was a stranger to those people. (51)

51) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr al-Razi.

Messages

1- All the prophets were united in thought, speech, and direction. (The verses "اِنّى لكم رسول أمین" [Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger] and "فاتّقوا اللّه و اَطیعون" [So fear Allah and obey me] are repeated in all the stories of this Surah, and the messages of these verses are the same as the messages of verses 160 to 164.)

Ayahs

۞ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْكَيْلَ وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْمُخْسِرِينَ (۱۸۱)
Observe the full measure, and do not be of those who give short measure. (181)
وَزِنُوا۟ بِٱلْقِسْطَاسِ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمِ (۱۸۲)
Weigh with an even balance, (182)
وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ (۱۸۳)
and do not cheat the people of their goods. Do not act wickedly on the earth, causing corruption. (183)

Key Points

The word Kayl (كیل) is used for liquids, the word Qisṭās (قِسطاس) is used for weights, and the word Ashyā'ihim (اشیائهم) is used for countable things and others. In these verses, we observe two commands and two prohibitions that complement each other: Awfū (اَوفوا - fulfill) and wa lā takūnū (و لاتكونوا - and do not be), and zinū (زنوا - weigh) and lā tabkhasū (لاتبخسوا - do not diminish).

It is stated in narrations: Those who do not inform the customer of the defect in their goods, the angels curse them, and those who deceive others through cunning and trickery are the worst of people. (52)

Under-measuring (or shortchanging) is one of the great economic evils that brings all the calamities of unlawful earnings (luqmah ḥarām) in its wake. Under-measuring is not limited to trade and transaction; rather, individuals may also under-measure in teaching, medicine, supervision, engineering, and other aspects of life, failing to fulfill the rights of others as they should.

52) Ghurar al-Ḥikam.

Messages

1- Measuring and weighing have a very long history. Awfū al-Kayl (اَوفوا الكَیل)

2- Under-measuring is forbidden (ḥarām), and observing the rights of the people (Ḥaqq an-Nās) is obligatory (wājib). Awfū al-Kayl (اَوفوا الكَیل) ...

3- The Prophets supervised the health of the community's economic system. Awfū al-Kayl (اَوفوا الكَیل)

4- Heavenly religions emphasize social and economic justice. Awfū (اَوفوا) - wa zinū (وَ زِنوا)

5- The standard of measurement in every matter must be just and correct. Al-Qisṭās al-Mustaqīm (القِسطاس المستقیم)

6- Observing the rights of all people is necessary, not just the believers. Lā tabkhasū an-Nās (لاتبخسوا النّاس)

7- Under-measuring is forbidden in everything and is not limited to measures and scales. Lā tabkhasū an-Nās ashyā'ahum (لاتبخسوا النّاس اشیائهم)

8- Under-measuring is a type of corruption, and those who inflict economic damage upon society are corrupters in the land (mufsidūn fī al-arḍ). This is because economic instability is the source of the disintegration of the social order. Lā ta‘thaw fī al-arḍ mufsidīn (لا تَعثوا فى الارض مفسدین)

9- Recognizing the existing deviations in society is the first step toward reforming society. (All verses)

Ayahs

وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَكُمْ وَٱلْجِبِلَّةَ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۸٤)
Be wary of Him who created you and the earlier generations.’ (184)
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسَحَّرِينَ (۱۸٥)
They said, ‘Indeed you are one of the bewitched. (185)
وَمَآ أَنتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا وَإِن نَّظُنُّكَ لَمِنَ ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (۱۸٦)
You are just a human being like us, and we indeed consider you to be a liar. (186)

Key Points

The word "جِبِلّ" (Jibill) means nation (Ummah) or community (Jama'at), just as Ayah 62 of Surah Ya Sin states: «و لقد اضلّ منكم جبلاّ كثیراً» (Satan led astray a great multitude of you). Since the word "جَبَل" (Jabal) means mountain and "جِبلّى" (Jibilli) means innate or natural, the term "جِبِلّى" is applied to nations and generations that are ancient, or those who possess an innate character and are steadfast like a mountain.

Messages

1- The One who created humanity is worthy of respect, and one must fear Him. «واتّقوا الّذى خلقكم»

2- Piety (Taqwa) and faith prevent economic corruption. The One who created you also provides your sustenance. «و اتّقوا الّذى خلقكم» (Considering the preceding verses regarding short-changing/under-measuring).

3- We should not rely on the corrupt customs and traditions of our ancestors, for we are all creatures of Allah and must obey Him. «خلقكم و الجبلّة الاوّلین»

4- Slander (or accusation) is the leverage used by those who lack logic. «قالوا انّما انت من المسحّرین»

5- The wrongdoer justifies his wrongdoing. (Instead of admitting his mistake and stubbornness, he considers the prophets to be bewitched). «انت من المسحّرین»

Ayahs

فَأَسْقِطْ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ (۱۸۷)
Make a fragment of the sky falls upon us, should you be truthful.’ (187)
قَالَ رَبِّىٓ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۸۸)
He said, ‘My Lord knows best what you are doing.’ (188)
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمْ عَذَابُ يَوْمِ ٱلظُّلَّةِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱۸۹)
So they impugned him, and then they were overtaken by the punishment of the day of the overshadowing cloud. It was indeed the punishment of a terrible day. (189)
إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً ۖ وَمَا كَانَ أَكْثَرُهُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۹٠)
There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them do not have faith. (190)
وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۱۹۱)
Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (191)

Key Points

The word kisaf (كِسَف), which appears four times in the Quran, is the plural of kisfah (كسفة), meaning "piece" or "fragment," and here it refers to a piece of cloud. The meaning of the "Torment of the Day of the Shade" (‘adhāb yawm al-ẓullah) is either the torment on the day when people sought refuge under shelters due to intense heat, or the day when a cloud cast a shadow over them. Several expressions have been used regarding the nature of the torment of the people of Shu'ayb: In one place, He states that they were destroyed by an earthquake (53). In another place, He states that they were tormented by a cry (ṣayḥah) (54). And here, the issue of the cloud is raised. All three matters can be reconciled: that thunder and a cry arose from a black cloud, causing them to tremble, or that they were destroyed simultaneously with the occurrence of an earthquake. And perhaps the earthquake relates to the People of Madyan (Aṣḥāb Madyan) and the black cloud relates to the People of the Wood (Aṣḥāb al-Aykah), because Prophet Shu'ayb was sent to both nations. In Sūrat al-A'rāf, his mission to the people of Madyan is mentioned, who were destroyed by an earthquake, but in this Sūrah, his mission to the People of the Wood is recalled.

53) A'rāf, 89. 54) Hūd, 96.

Messages

1- The demands and requests of the hard-hearted are also harsh. «اِسقط علینا كِسَفاً من السماء» 2- With the resistance of the leaders of truth, opponents retreat from their harsh slogans. The people of Shu'ayb first called him bewitched and a liar, then they said: If you are among the truthful. «انّما انت من المسحّرین» (You are only one of the bewitched) changes to «ان كنت من الصادقین» (If you are among the truthful). 3- In the method of propagation and guidance, it is not necessary to act according to the inclinations and demands of the opponents. «قال ربّى اعلم...» (He said: My Lord knows best...) (They were requesting the falling of fragments from the sky, but the Prophet replies: Allah is more knowledgeable.) 4- The remembrance of Allah and reliance upon Him is the best support against superstitions. «قال ربّى اعلم...» 5- Our destiny is in our own hands. «فكذّبوه فأخذهم ...» (So they denied him, and the torment seized them...) 6- Allah supports the prophets and destroys their opponents. «فكذّبوه فأخذهم» 7- Love of the world and the pursuit of wealth compel man to rebel against the prophets. «لاتبخسوا النّاس اشیائهم» (Do not defraud people of their goods) - «فكذّبوه» (So they denied him). 8- The Quran's goal in narrating history is to provide lessons, not storytelling. «لآیةً» (Surely there is a sign in that.) 9- Most people do not follow the path of truth. «و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین» 10- A mentor must possess compassion alongside power. «لهو العزیز الرّحیم» (He is indeed the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.) 11- The same Allah Who supported Shu'ayb and destroyed his opponents is also your Lord. «اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز الرّحیم» (Indeed, your Lord is the All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.) (The narration of the history of figures like Prophet Shu'ayb is both a source of comfort and consolation for the Prophet and a threat to the disbelievers and enemies.) 12- The destruction of the opponents of the prophets is a manifestation of Divine Might and power, just as the respite granted to them is a manifestation of His Mercy. «العزیز الرّحیم» (The All-Mighty, the All-Merciful.)

Ayahs

وَإِنَّهُۥ لَتَنزِيلُ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱۹۲)
This is indeed [a Book] sent down by the Lord of all the worlds, (192)
نَزَلَ بِهِ ٱلرُّوحُ ٱلْأَمِينُ (۱۹۳)
brought down by the Trustworthy Spirit (193)
عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُنذِرِينَ (۱۹٤)
upon your heart (so that you may be one of the warners), (194)
بِلِسَانٍ عَرَبِىٍّ مُّبِينٍ (۱۹٥)
in a clear Arabic language. (195)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لَفِى زُبُرِ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱۹٦)
It is indeed [foretold] in the scriptures of the ancients. (196)

Key Points

"Zubur" (زُبُر) comes from the word "zabr" (زَبر), meaning writing, and is the plural of "Zabur" (زبور), meaning book. We should not view the Qur'an simply, because: A: Its source is "Rabb al-'Alamin" (Lord of the Worlds). B: Its intermediary is "Ruh al-Amin" (the Trustworthy Spirit). C: Its vessel is the pure heart of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, "qalbika" (your heart). D: Its goal is the awakening of the people, "al-Mundhirin" (the warners). E: Its language is eloquent and clear, "Arabiyyun Mubin" (a clear Arabic). F: Its glad tidings are found in the books of the ancients, "Zubur al-Awwalin" (the scriptures of the ancients). Prophets Isma'il, Hud, Salih, and Shu'ayb علیهم السلام spoke the Arabic language. (55) The Angel of Revelation has several names: Ruh al-Amin, Ruh al-Qudus (the Holy Spirit), Shadid al-Quwa (Mighty in Power), Rasulun Karim (a Noble Messenger), and Jibra'il (Gabriel). The reason Jibra'il is called "Ruh" (Spirit) is either because the religion and the spirit of the people are revived through him, or because he Himself is a spiritual being.

55) Tafsir Manhaj as-Sadiqin.

Messages

1- The Qur'an is Divine Revelation, not hearsay or fabrications of the Prophet. «اِنّه لتنزیل»

2- The Qur'an has been sent down from the world of the unseen (ghayb) to the world of the manifest (shuhud). «لتنزیل ربّ العالمین»

3- The One who sends the revelation is the same One who manages and nurtures all existence. (Heavenly laws are consistent with the system of creation.) «لتنزیل ربّ العالمین»

4- The descent of revelation is a means of growth and is among the affairs of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «لتنزیل ربّ العالمین»

5- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is to employ intermediaries. «نَزَل به الرّوح الامین»

6- The Divine Revelation has been sent down without any addition or omission. «نَزَل به الرّوح الامین»

7- The heart is the center for receiving truths. «على قلبك »

8- Unless we believe something from the depths of our soul, we cannot give a serious warning. «على قلبك لتكون من المنذرین»

9- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to send Prophets for warning and admonition. «من المنذرین»

10- The cure for the heedless is through warning and admonition. «من المنذرین» In the Qur'an, warning (indhar) is mentioned more often than glad tidings (bisharah).

11- Propagation must be clear and in the language of the people. «عربىّ مبین»

12- The glad tidings of the Qur'an were mentioned in previous heavenly books. «زُبرالاوّلین» Just as the glad tidings of the coming of the Prophet of Islam were in the Torah and the Gospel. «یجدونه مكتوباً فى التوراة والانجیل» (56)

13- In planning and management, prepare the ground for the growth, guidance, and activity of future generations. «زُبرالاوّلین»

14- The theme and content of the Qur'an were present in previous heavenly books, but its specific wording (alfaz) was revealed upon the blessed heart of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. «زُبرالاوّلین»

56) اعراف، ۱۷۵.

Ayahs

أَوَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُمْ ءَايَةً أَن يَعْلَمَهُۥ عُلَمَـٰٓؤُا۟ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ (۱۹۷)
Is it not a sign for them that the learned of the Children of Israel recognize it? (197)
وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنَـٰهُ عَلَىٰ بَعْضِ ٱلْأَعْجَمِينَ (۱۹۸)
Had We sent it down upon some non-Arab (198)
فَقَرَأَهُۥ عَلَيْهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ مُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۹۹)
and had he recited it to them, they would not have believed in it. (199)

Key Points

| Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The worth and virtue of non-Arabs lies in the fact that they inclined toward the Quran, which is in the Arabic language. However, if the Quran had been in a non-Arabic language, some Arabs would not have believed. (57)

57) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq.

Messages

1- The excuse of the negligent ignorant person is not accepted. «أولم یكن لهم آیة» Since you do not ask your scholars, you deserve reproach and blame.

2- The scholars of the Children of Israel were aware of the truthfulness of the Quran. «یعلمه علماء بنى اسرائیل»

3- In propagation and education, pay attention to the national sentiments and language of the audience. «لو نزّلناه على بعض الاعجمین...»

4- Fanaticism and racism prevent the acceptance of truth. «لو نزّلناه - ما كانوا به مؤمنین»

5- Fanaticism and nationalism are prevalent among the Arab people. «لو نزّلناه... ما كانوا به مؤمنین»

Ayahs

كَذَٰلِكَ سَلَكْنَـٰهُ فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۲٠٠)
This is how We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty: (200)
لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ (۲٠۱)
they do not believe in it until they sight the painful punishment. (201)
فَيَأْتِيَهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۲٠۲)
It will overtake them suddenly while they are unaware. (202)
فَيَقُولُوا۟ هَلْ نَحْنُ مُنظَرُونَ (۲٠۳)
Thereupon they will say, ‘Shall we be granted any respite?’ (203)
أَفَبِعَذَابِنَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (۲٠٤)
So do they seek to hasten on Our punishment? (204)
أَفَرَءَيْتَ إِن مَّتَّعْنَـٰهُمْ سِنِينَ (۲٠٥)
Tell me, should We let them enjoy for some years, (205)
ثُمَّ جَآءَهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُوعَدُونَ (۲٠٦)
then there comes to them what they have been promised, (206)

Key Points

We read in narrations: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله saw in a dream that Banu Umayyah were climbing His pulpit and misleading the people. When He awoke from the sleep, He was saddened. At this time, Gabriel brought these verses: «أفراَیت اِن متّعناهم سِنین. ثمّ جائهم ما كانوا یُوعدون»(58)

58) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Allah completes the argument (proof) even against the disbelievers, presenting the Quran to them and causing it to pass through their hearts. «سَلَكناه»

2- If the capacity (for acceptance) is absent, the Quran will not be effective. «سَلكناه فى قلوب المجرمین لایؤمنون»

3- Crime and sin erase the ground for guidance within a person. «المجرمین لایؤمنون»

4- Just as the polytheist Arabs of Mecca would not have believed if the Quran had been revealed in a non-Arabic language, similarly, even now that it is revealed in clear Arabic and its contents enter their hearts, they still do not believe. «لا یؤمنون به»

5- Faith at the moment of distress is useless. «لا یؤمنون به حتّى یَروا العذاب»

6- We should not be deceived by the respite granted by Allah, because death and divine punishment come suddenly. «فیأتیهم بَغتَةً»

7- The criminal, at the moment of death, requests a respite. «هل نحن مُنظَرون»

8- One day, dormant consciences will awaken. «هل نحن مُنظَرون»

9- Those who hasten the sight of divine punishment will one day ask for a respite to be safe from the punishment. «مُنظَرون - یستعجلون»

10- Respite is useless for the black-hearted. «مَتّعناهم سِنین... جائهم ما كانوا یوعدون»

Ayahs

مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يُمَتَّعُونَ (۲٠۷)
of what avail to them will be that which they were given to enjoy? (207)
وَمَآ أَهْلَكْنَا مِن قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا لَهَا مُنذِرُونَ (۲٠۸)
We have not destroyed any town without its having warners, (208)
ذِكْرَىٰ وَمَا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِينَ (۲٠۹)
for the sake of admonition, and We were not unjust. (209)

Key Points

Allah destroys every misguided individual or nation only after He has warned them beforehand. Otherwise, punishment without warning is oppression (zulm), and oppression is not befitting of Allah. The Quran has stated this truth in various verses: * «و ما ظلمناهم» (59) We did not oppress them. * «و ما اللّه یُرید ظلما» (60) Allah does not intend oppression. * «ما كان اللّه لیَظلمهم» (61) It is not befitting of Allah to oppress them. * «لا یظلمون نَقیرا» (62), «لا یظلمون شیئا» (63) Not the slightest injustice will be done to them. * «لا یظلمون فَتیلا» (64) Allah does not oppress even the measure of the thread in the date seed or less than that. * «لا یظلم مِثقال ذَرّة» (65) Allah does not commit injustice even the weight of an atom.

59) An-Nahl, 118. 60) Ghafir, 31. 61) Al-Ankabut, 40. 62) An-Nisa, 124. 63) Maryam, 60. 64) An-Nisa, 49. 65) An-Nisa, 40.

Messages

1- At the time of Divine wrath, all means of enjoyment are useless. «ما أغنى عنهم»

2- Do not be saddened by the prosperity of the opponents of religion, for We shall take it away one day. «ما أغنى عنهم ما كانوا یُمتّعون»

3- Divine wrath occurs after the completion of the proof (Hujjah). Punishment must follow admonition. «و ما اهلكنا... الاّ لها منذرون»

4- In every nation, there are the friends of Allah (Awliya') who warn them. «من قریةالاّ لهامنذرون»

5- The duty of the prophets is to warn, and they are not responsible for the people's acceptance or rejection. «منذرون»

6- Man inherently understands the truths, but sometimes heedlessness overcomes him, and the cure for heedlessness is remembrance and warning. «منذرون»

7- Punishment before admonition is oppression. «ذِكرى و ما كنّا ظالمین»

8- The Divine station is far removed from oppression. «و ما كنّا ظالمین»

Ayahs

وَمَا تَنَزَّلَتْ بِهِ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينُ (۲۱٠)
It has not been brought down by the devils. (210)
وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لَهُمْ وَمَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ (۲۱۱)
Neither does it behoove them, nor are they capable [of doing that]. (211)
إِنَّهُمْ عَنِ ٱلسَّمْعِ لَمَعْزُولُونَ (۲۱۲)
Indeed, they are kept at bay [even] from hearing it. (212)
فَلَا تَدْعُ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُعَذَّبِينَ (۲۱۳)
So do not invoke any god besides Allah, lest you should be among the punished. (213)

Key Points

Revelation is of two types: A: Divine revelation, which descends upon the heart of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله through the Trustworthy Spirit (Ruh al-Amin). This type of revelation is neither fitting for the jinn and devils, nor do they have the ability to receive or transmit it. «و ما یَنبغى لهم و ما یَستَطیعون» B: The other type of revelation consists of satanic whispers and inspirations that the devils cast upon their allies. «اِنّ الشیاطین لَیُوحون الى اولیائهم»(66)

The Quran possesses firmness, a lofty status, sanctity, and special protection, because its bearer is the Trustworthy Spirit (Ruh al-Amin). It is safeguarded from any tampering, «لمَعزولون» (they are surely isolated/removed). Its recipient is infallible, «و مایَنطق عن الهَوى»(67) (nor does he speak out of [personal] desire). And it is guaranteed. «اِنّا نحن نَزّلنا الذّكر واِنّا له لحافظون»(68)

The polytheists proposed to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: If you call upon our idols and respect them, we will make you our leader. The Ayah descended to disappoint the disbelievers. «فلاتَدعُ مع اللّه»

66) Al-An'am, 121. 67) An-Najm, 3. 68) Al-Hijr, 9.

Messages

1- Respond decisively to accusations. «و ما تَنزّلت» (And it was not sent down). The polytheists assumed the casting of the verses was the work of devils.

2- The sanctity of revelation is far removed from the wickedness of the devils. «ما یَنبغى لهم»

3- The power of the jinn and devils is limited. «و ما یَستطیعون»

4- The entry of outsiders into sacred centers is forbidden. «اِنّهم عن السَمعِ لمعزولون»

5- If there is capability, purity, and capacity, it compels the angel to descend. «نَزَل به الرّوح الامین على قلبك» (The Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down upon your heart). But if there is no readiness, even flying toward the heavens will be accompanied by repulsion and deprivation. «لمعزولون», «فاَتبَعه شهاب»(69)

6- The ultimate goal of the Quran's descent is Monotheism (Tawhid). «و ماتَنزّلت... فلاتَدعُ مع اللّه»

7- Allah forbids the Prophet from polytheism so that we may learn; otherwise, the Prophet never turned to anyone other than Allah for a moment. (They speak to the door so the wall may hear.) «فلا تدعُ مع اللّه»

8- The infallibility (Ismah) of the Prophets does not contradict their freedom and choice. (The Prophet is infallible, but He could turn to someone other than Allah, hence He is subject to the prohibition.) «فلاتَدعُ»

9- The leaders of Monotheism must themselves be the first monotheists. «فلا تَدعُ»

10- Anything or anyone other than Allah is not worthy of worship. «الهاً آخر»

11- Polytheism has caused the destruction of previous nations. «فلاتدع... فتكون من المعذّبین» (Meaning, you would become one of those previously punished.)

12- Turning to anyone other than Allah leads to misery and punishment. «فتكون من المعذّبین»

13- The Divine verses issue warnings even to great personalities. «فتكون من المعذّبین»

69) Al-Hijr, 18.

Ayahs

وَأَنذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ ٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ (۲۱٤)
Warn the nearest of your kinsfolk, (214)
وَٱخْفِضْ جَنَاحَكَ لِمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۲۱٥)
and lower your wing to the faithful who follow you. (215)
فَإِنْ عَصَوْكَ فَقُلْ إِنِّى بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۲۱٦)
But if they disobey you, say, ‘I am absolved of what you do.’ (216)
وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ (۲۱۷)
And put your trust in the All-mighty, the All-merciful, (217)

Key Points

Following the revelation of Ayah 214, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله invited His relatives to a feast. After the hospitality, He began His speech and forbade them from polytheism (shirk) and idol worship. (70) The word 'ashīrah (عَشیرة) is perhaps used for relatives because of the association (mu'āsharah) of kin with one another. In doing good to others (such as visiting, spending [in charity], lending, guiding, and enjoining good), one's close relatives take precedence. "And warn your nearest kindred" (اَنذر عَشیرتك الاَقربین).

Spreading the wing (lowering the wing) is mentioned in the Quran in two contexts: one concerning the Prophet for the believers: "And lower your wing to those who follow you" (واخفض جَناحك لمَن اتّبعك); and the other concerning the child for the parents. However, in the case of the child, the word dhull (ذلّ - humility/submissiveness) is also included: "And lower to them the wing of humility out of mercy" (واخفض لهما جَناح الذّل من الرّحمة). (71)

In the Ayah "And warn your kindred" (واَنذر عَشیرتك), women were also among the addressees. 'Ashīratuk (عشیرتك) includes both men and women, and Tafsir al-Mizan quotes Al-Tabarani that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله had also invited the women.

70) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 71) Al-Isra', 24.

Messages

1- The bond of kinship results in greater responsibility. "And warn your kindred" (و اَنذر عشیرتك).

2- In the school of revelation, all people are equal before the law of Allah, and even the Prophet's relatives are subject to warning. "And warn your kindred" (و اَنذر عشیرتك).

3- In propagation (tabligh) and forbidding evil, first start with yourself; "So do not call upon any other god with Allah" (فلاتَدعُ مع اللّه), then your relatives. "And warn your kindred" (و اَنذر عشیرتك).

4- Guidance and propagation must be explicit. "And warn" (و اَنذِر).

5 - Family relations should not prevent forbidding evil. "And warn" (و اَنذِر).

6- Warning one's relatives strengthens the ground for others to accept the truth. (People will say: He speaks these words even to His own kin). "And warn your kindred" (و اَنذِر عشیرتك).

7- Priority is also relevant among relatives. "The nearest" (الاقربین).

8 - Among relatives, due to deep knowledge and trust, the ground for accepting the truth is greater. "The nearest" (الاقربین).

9- Warning must be accompanied by fervor and affection. "Warn... and lower" (اَنذِر... واخفض).

10- Humility before companions, followers, and subordinates is a necessary trait for a leader and propagator. "And lower your wing to those who follow you" (واخفِض جَناحك لمَن اتّبعك).

11- Humility towards the believers, dissociation from the polytheists. "And lower - So say: Indeed, I am innocent" (واخفِض - فقل اِنّى برى ء).

12- It is sufficient proof of the importance of humility that Allah commands the Prophet to practice it. "And lower" (واخفض).

13- The prerequisite for believing in Allah is following the Prophet. "To those who follow you of the believers" (لمَن اتّبعك من المؤمنین), and He did not say: "To the believers" (للمؤمنین).

14- Believers hold such a high status that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is commanded to be humble before them. "And lower your wing to those who follow you of the believers" (واخفِض جناحك لمَن اتّبعك من المؤمنین).

15- The criterion for interactions is faith and disbelief, not kinship. (Warn your relatives, but be humble towards the believers). "Warn - And lower" (اَنذر - واخفِض).

16- Humility is not only verbal; practical modesty is also necessary. "Your wing" (جَناحك).

17- The measure of tawalli (allegiance) and tabarri (dissociation) is obedience to the Messenger of Allah. "They follow you - They disobey you" (اتّبعك - عَصوك).

18- Internal aversion to disbelief is not enough; verbal declaration is necessary. "So say" (فقُل).

19- One who relies upon Allah (has tawakkul) can declare dissociation and aversion from the actions of the disbelievers and the indifference of relatives. "Innocent - And rely" (بَرى ء - و توكّل).

20- We should rely upon Him Who is Powerful and loves us. "And rely upon the Exalted in Might, the Merciful" (وتوكّل على العزیزالرّحیم).

Ayahs

ٱلَّذِى يَرَىٰكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ (۲۱۸)
who sees you when you stand [for prayer], (218)
وَتَقَلُّبَكَ فِى ٱلسَّـٰجِدِينَ (۲۱۹)
and your going about among those who prostrate. (219)
إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۲۲٠)
Indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (220)
هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ عَلَىٰ مَن تَنَزَّلُ ٱلشَّيَـٰطِينُ (۲۲۱)
Should I inform you on whom the devils descend? (221)
تَنَزَّلُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيمٍ (۲۲۲)
They descend on every sinful liar. (222)
يُلْقُونَ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ كَـٰذِبُونَ (۲۲۳)
They eavesdrop, and most of them are liars. (223)

Key Points

The word affāk means a great liar and fabricator of falsehoods. Regarding the rising (qiyām) of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in Ayah 218, the intent is either rising for prayer, or rising for warning and propagation. As Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: the intent is rising in the matter of prophethood. It is probable that the intent of Ayah 223 is this: the devils who descend upon corrupt individuals lend their ears to hear the news of the heavens, even though they are repelled by meteors. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The intent of the phrase تَقلّبك فى السّاجدین (Your movement among those who prostrate) is that Allah was aware of your movement through the loins of your God-worshipping ancestors.(72) Since the polytheists imagined the descent of revelation to be the descent of devils upon the Prophet, Allah states decisively: devils descend upon sinful fabricators of falsehoods, not upon the infallible Prophet. Therefore, we have two types of descent: one is the descent of revelation through a trustworthy angel: انّه لتَنزیل ربّ العالمین نزل به الرّوح الامین (Indeed, it is the revelation of the Lord of the worlds, brought down by the Trustworthy Spirit); the other is the descent of devils upon corrupt individuals: تَنزّل على كلّ اَفّاك اَثیم (They descend upon every sinful fabricator of falsehoods).

72) Bihar, Vol. 15, p. 3.

Messages

  1. Allah consoles His Prophet by reminding him of His supervision. یَراك... (He sees you...)

  2. A person is under the special attention of Allah while praying. یَراك حین تَقوم (He sees you when you stand up)

  3. Prostration (sujūd) is the most important pillar of prayer. فى السّاجدین (among those who prostrate)

  4. We should rely upon a God Who, besides possessing Might and Mercy, is Hearing and Knowing, and sees our movements and acts of worship. توكّل على العزیز الرّحیم - السّمیع العلیم (Rely upon the Mighty, the Merciful — the Hearing, the Knowing)

  5. In the method of propagation, we should create a context for attention and listening among the audience by asking questions. هل اُنبّئكم (Shall I inform you?)

  6. Different spirits, capacities, and merits lead to different types of descents. نَزَل به الرّوح الامین على قلبك (The Trustworthy Spirit brought it down upon your heart) — تَنزّل على كلّ اَفّاك اَثیم (They descend upon every sinful fabricator of falsehoods).

  7. The recipient of revelation is only one heart, the pure heart of the Prophet (قلبك), but the recipient of satanic whispers is every polluted heart. كلّ افّاك (every fabricator of falsehoods).

  8. Worse than sin is its repetition and becoming ingrained in a person's character. افّاك اَثیم (sinful fabricator of falsehoods).

  9. The sign of a polluted sinner is that he lends his ear to every word. یُلقُونَ السمع (They lend their hearing).

  10. Lying is the source of many sins and is more dangerous than them; thus, the heart that fabricates falsehoods becomes the landing place for Satan. تَنزّل على كلّ افّاك اَثیم (They descend upon every sinful fabricator of falsehoods).

Ayahs

وَٱلشُّعَرَآءُ يَتَّبِعُهُمُ ٱلْغَاوُۥنَ (۲۲٤)
As for the poets, [only] the perverse follow them. (224)
أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِى كُلِّ وَادٍ يَهِيمُونَ (۲۲٥)
Have you not regarded that they rove in every valley, (225)
وَأَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ مَا لَا يَفْعَلُونَ (۲۲٦)
and that they say what they do not do? (226)
إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَذَكَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا وَٱنتَصَرُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا ظُلِمُوا۟ ۗ وَسَيَعْلَمُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَىَّ مُنقَلَبٍ يَنقَلِبُونَ (۲۲۷)
Barring those who have faith, do righteous deeds, and remember Allah much often, and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged. And the wrongdoers will soon know at what goal they will end up. (227)

Key Points

The word ghāwūn (غاوون) comes from ghayy (غىّ), which is the opposite of rushd (guidance/maturity), just as He states elsewhere: «قد تَبیّن الرّشد من الغَىّ» (73)

Since the disbelievers considered the Qur'an to be a product of illusion and imagination, and the Prophet to be a poet, these verses condemn them by stating that the followers of the poets are the misguided ones, but the followers of the Prophet of Islam are not misguided. Poets are aimless and bewildered and do not act upon their own words, but the Prophet is not like this. The harmony between the speech and conduct of the Prophet of Islam is a sign that he is not a poet.

It is narrated from Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq عليهما السلام that, regarding the verse «والشعراء یَتبعهم الغاوون» (And the poets—[only] the misguided follow them), they said: Those who have learned jurisprudence (fiqh) for reasons other than religion, or have not learned it deeply, are both misguided and misguide others. Storytellers are also included in this censure. (74)

Since the names of aimless poets are mentioned alongside the fabricators of lies upon whom the devils descend, perhaps a connection can be discovered between the lying devil and the aimless, frivolous poet.

According to narrations: Dhikr Kathīr (frequent remembrance) is the Tasbīhāt of Hadrat Zahra عليها السلام, meaning thirty-four times «اللّه اكبر» (Allahu Akbar), thirty-three times «الحمداللّه» (Alhamdulillah), and thirty-three times «سبحان اللّه» (Subhan Allah).

It is stated in a hadith: The remembrance of Allah is not only with the tongue, but it is the remembrance of God when abandoning sin and performing worship. «ذكروا اللّه» (75)

We read in a hadith: The blessed head of Imam Husayn عليه السلام recited this verse while mounted on the spear: «و سیَعلم الّذین ظلموا...» (And those who have wronged will soon know...) And Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The clear manifestation of oppression is oppression against the Family of Muhammad عليهم السلام. (76)

Poetry and Poets

We present some points about poetry and poets in connection with the final verses of this Surah:

  • It is narrated from Tafsir Baydawi: Since the poetry of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) mostly revolved around fantasies, descriptions of beautiful women, flirtation, vain boasts, or slander and violation of others' honor, these verses were revealed. «والشعراء یَتبعهم الغاوُون»
  • The difference between a sage (hakīm) and a poet is that the sage first considers the meanings and then employs the words, whereas the poet first considers the form and the words, and then expresses the meanings. (77)
  • In narrations, good poetry that defends the truth is considered superior to fighting with a spearhead and has been praised. (78)
  • The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Some forms of expression are like magic, and some poems are wisdom. (79)
  • The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله told a committed poet named "Hassān": The Holy Spirit is with you. (80)
  • During one of his journeys, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله called upon "Hassān" to recite poetry. He recited, and the Prophet listened. The Prophet ordered that a special place be designated for "Hassān" in the mosque. (81)
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام used to tell his companions: Familiarize your children with the poetry of "Abdī," for he is a good and principled poet. (82)
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whoever composes one couplet of poetry in defense of our truthfulness, Allah will grant him a house in Paradise. (83)
  • Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Reciting poetry is makrūh (disliked) for a fasting person, or for someone in the state of Ihrām (consecration) or in the sacred precinct (Haram), and reciting poetry on the day or night of Friday is also disliked. (84)

  • We read in narrations: The truest verse of poetry during the Age of Ignorance was this:

    الا كلّ شى ء ما خلا اللّه باطل و كلّ نعیم لا محالة زائل (85)

    Meaning: Know that everything, except Allah, is vain, and every blessing will inevitably perish sooner or later.

  • It is stated in a narration: As soon as the verse «والشعراء یَتبعهم...» was revealed, a group of Muslim poets became concerned about themselves and came to the Prophet. He said: «اِنّ المؤمن مجاهد بسیفه و لسانه» (Indeed, the believer strives with his sword and his tongue), meaning the believer strives with his sword and his tongue. (86)

Messages

1- Attracting people is not important; what is important is which individuals are attracted and for what purpose. «یَتبعهم الغاوون»

2- Islam is not opposed to art; it is opposed to aimless poetry. «فى كلّ وادٍ یهیمون»

3- If poetry is not accompanied by faith and piety, the personal fantasies and feelings of the poet and the desires of the people will drag the poet into a different valley every day. «فى كلّ واد یهیمون»

5- The afflictions of poetry are several things: the following of the unworthy, «یتبعهم الغاوُون»; aimlessness, «فى كلّ واد یهیمون»; and lack of action, «یقولون ما لایفعلون».

6- If poetry and art are not placed alongside faith, they will become a suitable ground and a smooth path for the movement of the deviated. «والشعراء یَتبعهم الغاوُون - الاّ الّذین آمنوا»

7- One of the methods of Amr bil Ma'rūf (enjoining good) and Nahy anil Munkar (forbidding evil) is introducing models of truth and falsehood. «والشعراء ... الاّ الّذین»

8- The Qur'an criticizes the poet who is a man of words, not deeds. «یقولون ما لایفعلون»

9- Observe fairness. Do not ignore the rights of the righteous in any class or group. Poetry and art are valuable in the hands of the righteous. «الاّ الّذین آمنوا»

10- Faith, righteous deeds, and the remembrance of Allah save the artist and the poet from deviation. «الاّ الّذین آمنوا ...»

11- The Qur'an approves of poetry that comes from a believing, active, and God-remembering poet, and is used to motivate people to defend the truth. «آمنوا و عملوا ... و انتصروا...»

12- Faith precedes action, and actions must also be righteous in order to be effective. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

13- The remembrance of Allah is not limited by time, place, or quantity. (At all times, in all places, as much as possible) «ذكروا اللّه كثیراً»

14- Wherever the danger of slipping is great, the remembrance of Allah must also be great. «ذكروا اللّه كثیراً»

15- Let us confront the oppressors with art and poetry. «وانتصروا من بعد ما ظلموا»

16- Allah is lying in wait for the oppressors. «و سیعلم الّذین ظلموا»
